   791→    // Database Optimization - Feature: Database Structure Optimization
   792→
   793→    /**
   794→     * Find latest vulnerabilities per asset (current vulnerabilities only)
   795→     * Optimized database query that replaces in-memory filtering
   796→     * Uses indexes: idx_vulnerability_asset, idx_vulnerability_scan_timestamp
   797→     *
   798→     * Feature: Database Structure Optimization
   799→     *
   800→     * This query finds vulnerabilities from the latest scan for each asset using a subquery.
   801→     * Much more efficient than loading all vulnerabilities and filtering in memory.
   802→     *
   803→     * @return List of vulnerabilities from the latest scan per asset
   804→     */
   805→    @io.micronaut.data.annotation.Query(
   806→        """
   807→        SELECT v FROM Vulnerability v
   808→        WHERE (v.asset.id, v.importTimestamp) IN (
   809→            SELECT v2.asset.id, MAX(v2.importTimestamp)
   810→            FROM Vulnerability v2
   811→            GROUP BY v2.asset.id
   812→        )
   813→        ORDER BY v.importTimestamp DESC
   814→        """
   815→    )
   816→    fun findLatestVulnerabilitiesPerAsset(): List<Vulnerability>
   817→
   818→    /**
   819→     * Find latest vulnerabilities per asset with pagination
   820→     * Optimized for paginated views of current vulnerabilities
   821→     * Uses indexes: idx_vulnerability_asset, idx_vulnerability_scan_timestamp
   822→     *
   823→     * Feature: Database Structure Optimization
   824→     *
   825→     * @param pageable Pagination parameters
   826→     * @return Page of vulnerabilities from latest scan per asset
   827→     */
   828→    @io.micronaut.data.annotation.Query(
   829→        value = """
   830→        SELECT v FROM Vulnerability v
   831→        WHERE (v.asset.id, v.importTimestamp) IN (
   832→            SELECT v2.asset.id, MAX(v2.importTimestamp)
   833→            FROM Vulnerability v2
   834→            GROUP BY v2.asset.id
   835→        )
   836→        ORDER BY v.importTimestamp DESC
   837→        """,
   838→        countQuery = """
   839→        SELECT COUNT(v) FROM Vulnerability v
   840→        WHERE (v.asset.id, v.importTimestamp) IN (
   841→            SELECT v2.asset.id, MAX(v2.importTimestamp)
   842→            FROM Vulnerability v2
   843→            GROUP BY v2.asset.id
   844→        )
   845→        """
   846→    )
   847→    fun findLatestVulnerabilitiesPerAsset(pageable: Pageable): Page<Vulnerability>
   848→
   849→    /**
   850→     * Find latest vulnerabilities per asset with filters
   851→     * Optimized query for filtered current vulnerability views
   852→     * Uses indexes: idx_vulnerability_asset, idx_vulnerability_severity, idx_vulnerability_scan_timestamp
   853→     *
   854→     * Feature: Database Structure Optimization
   855→     *
   856→     * @param severity Optional CVSS severity filter
   857→     * @param assetName Optional asset name filter (case-insensitive)
   858→     * @param productFilter Optional product version filter (case-insensitive)
   859→     * @param domainFilter Optional AD domain filter (case-insensitive)
   860→     * @param cloudAccountIdFilter Optional AWS cloud account ID filter (case-insensitive)
   861→     * @param pageable Pagination parameters
   862→     * @return Page of filtered current vulnerabilities
   863→     */
   864→    @io.micronaut.data.annotation.Query(
   865→        value = """
   866→        SELECT v FROM Vulnerability v
   867→        JOIN FETCH v.asset a
   868→        WHERE (:severity IS NULL OR v.cvssSeverity = :severity)
   869→        AND (:assetName IS NULL OR LOWER(a.name) LIKE LOWER(CONCAT('%', :assetName, '%')))
   870→        AND (:productFilter IS NULL OR LOWER(v.vulnerableProductVersions) LIKE LOWER(CONCAT('%', :productFilter, '%')))
   871→        AND (:domainFilter IS NULL OR a.adDomain LIKE CONCAT('%', :domainFilter, '%'))
   872→        AND (:cloudAccountIdFilter IS NULL OR a.cloudAccountId LIKE CONCAT('%', :cloudAccountIdFilter, '%'))
   873→        ORDER BY v.importTimestamp DESC, a.name ASC, v.vulnerabilityId ASC
   874→        """,
   875→        countQuery = """
   876→        SELECT COUNT(v) FROM Vulnerability v
   877→        WHERE (:severity IS NULL OR v.cvssSeverity = :severity)
   878→        AND (:assetName IS NULL OR LOWER(v.asset.name) LIKE LOWER(CONCAT('%', :assetName, '%')))
   879→        AND (:productFilter IS NULL OR LOWER(v.vulnerableProductVersions) LIKE LOWER(CONCAT('%', :productFilter, '%')))
   880→        AND (:domainFilter IS NULL OR v.asset.adDomain LIKE CONCAT('%', :domainFilter, '%'))
   881→        AND (:cloudAccountIdFilter IS NULL OR v.asset.cloudAccountId LIKE CONCAT('%', :cloudAccountIdFilter, '%'))
   882→        """
   883→    )
   884→    fun findLatestVulnerabilitiesPerAssetWithFilters(
   885→        severity: String?,
   886→        assetName: String?,
   887→        productFilter: String?,
   888→        domainFilter: String?,
   889→        cloudAccountIdFilter: String?,
   890→        pageable: Pageable
   891→    ): Page<Vulnerability>
   892→
   893→    // PERFORMANCE OPTIMIZATION: Batch queries for MaterializedViewRefreshService (Fix 3)
   894→
   895→    /**
   896→     * Find all vulnerabilities older than the threshold date
   897→     * Returns full Vulnerability entities with eagerly fetched Asset for batch processing
   898→     *
   899→     * Feature: Outdated Assets Performance Optimization (Fix 3)
   900→     * Replaces N+1 query pattern: Instead of querying per asset, get all overdue vulnerabilities at once
   901→     *
   902→     * @param thresholdDate Vulnerabilities with scanTimestamp before this date are considered overdue
   903→     * @return List of all overdue vulnerabilities with their assets
   904→     */
   905→    @io.micronaut.data.annotation.Query(
   906→        """
   907→        SELECT v FROM Vulnerability v
   908→        JOIN FETCH v.asset a
   909→        LEFT JOIN FETCH a.workgroups

   932→    // PERFORMANCE OPTIMIZATION: Database-level access control queries
   933→
   934→    /**
   935→     * Find latest vulnerabilities per asset with filters AND access control
   936→     * Pushes both filtering and access control to database level for true pagination
   937→     *
   938→     * Feature: Database Structure Optimization - Phase 2
   939→     * Replaces: In-memory filtering by accessible asset IDs
   940→     *
   941→     * This query is for NON-ADMIN users - filters by accessible asset IDs
   942→     * For ADMIN users, use findLatestVulnerabilitiesPerAssetWithFilters() instead
   943→     *
   944→     * @param accessibleAssetIds Set of asset IDs the user can access (from AssetFilterService)
   945→     * @param severity Optional CVSS severity filter
   946→     * @param assetName Optional asset name filter (case-insensitive)
   947→     * @param productFilter Optional product version filter (case-insensitive)
   948→     * @param domainFilter Optional AD domain filter (case-insensitive)
   949→     * @param cloudAccountIdFilter Optional AWS cloud account ID filter (case-insensitive)
   950→     * @param pageable Pagination parameters (NOT UNPAGED - use actual pagination!)
   951→     * @return Page of filtered current vulnerabilities with access control applied
   952→     */
   953→    @io.micronaut.data.annotation.Query(
   954→        value = """
   955→        SELECT v FROM Vulnerability v
   956→        JOIN FETCH v.asset a
   957→        WHERE a.id IN :accessibleAssetIds
   958→        AND (:severity IS NULL OR v.cvssSeverity = :severity)
   959→        AND (:assetName IS NULL OR LOWER(a.name) LIKE LOWER(CONCAT('%', :assetName, '%')))
   960→        AND (:productFilter IS NULL OR LOWER(v.vulnerableProductVersions) LIKE LOWER(CONCAT('%', :productFilter, '%')))
   961→        AND (:domainFilter IS NULL OR a.adDomain LIKE CONCAT('%', :domainFilter, '%'))
   962→        AND (:cloudAccountIdFilter IS NULL OR a.cloudAccountId LIKE CONCAT('%', :cloudAccountIdFilter, '%'))
   963→        ORDER BY v.importTimestamp DESC, a.name ASC, v.vulnerabilityId ASC
   964→        """,
   965→        countQuery = """
   966→        SELECT COUNT(v) FROM Vulnerability v
   967→        WHERE v.asset.id IN :accessibleAssetIds
   968→        AND (:severity IS NULL OR v.cvssSeverity = :severity)
   969→        AND (:assetName IS NULL OR LOWER(v.asset.name) LIKE LOWER(CONCAT('%', :assetName, '%')))
   970→        AND (:productFilter IS NULL OR LOWER(v.vulnerableProductVersions) LIKE LOWER(CONCAT('%', :productFilter, '%')))
   971→        AND (:domainFilter IS NULL OR v.asset.adDomain LIKE CONCAT('%', :domainFilter, '%'))
   972→        AND (:cloudAccountIdFilter IS NULL OR v.asset.cloudAccountId LIKE CONCAT('%', :cloudAccountIdFilter, '%'))
   973→        """
   974→    )
   975→    fun findLatestVulnerabilitiesWithAccessControl(
   976→        accessibleAssetIds: Set<Long>,
   977→        severity: String?,
   978→        assetName: String?,
   979→        productFilter: String?,
   980→        domainFilter: String?,
   981→        cloudAccountIdFilter: String?,
   982→        pageable: Pageable
   983→    ): Page<Vulnerability>

   997→    // Domain Vulnerabilities - Latest Import Only Filter
   998→
   999→    /**
  1000→     * Find vulnerabilities from the latest import per asset
  1001→     * Used for Domain Vulnerabilities to match CrowdStrike Lookup counts
  1002→     *
  1003→     * Filters to only include vulnerabilities from the most recent importTimestamp
  1004→     * for each asset, eliminating historical/duplicate records.
  1005→     *
  1006→     * Uses ROW_NUMBER() window function for reliable filtering that handles:
  1007→     * - Timestamp precision issues (Java nanoseconds vs MariaDB microseconds)
  1008→     * - NULL import_timestamp values (treated as oldest, excluded)
  1009→     * - Multiple vulnerabilities with exact same timestamp (all included)
  1010→     *
  1011→     * Feature: 053-crowdstrike-import-cleanup
  1012→     *
  1013→     * @param assetIds Set of asset IDs to query
  1014→     * @return List of vulnerabilities from the latest import per asset
  1015→     */
  1016→    @io.micronaut.data.annotation.Query(
  1017→        value = """
  1018→        SELECT v.* FROM vulnerability v
  1019→        WHERE v.id IN (
  1020→            SELECT id FROM (
  1021→                SELECT id, asset_id, import_timestamp,
  1022→                       DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY asset_id ORDER BY COALESCE(import_timestamp, '1970-01-01') DESC) as rnk
  1023→                FROM vulnerability
  1024→                WHERE asset_id IN :assetIds
  1025→            ) ranked
  1026→            WHERE rnk = 1
  1027→        )
  1028→        """,
  1029→        nativeQuery = true
  1030→    )
  1031→    fun findLatestVulnerabilitiesForAssetIds(assetIds: Set<Long>): List<Vulnerability>
  1032→
  1033→    /**
  1034→     * Count vulnerabilities per asset for latest import only
  1035→     * Used for efficient count queries without loading all vulnerability data
  1036→     *
  1037→     * @param assetIds Set of asset IDs to query
  1038→     * @return Map-like result of asset_id to vulnerability count
  1039→     */
  1040→    @io.micronaut.data.annotation.Query(
  1041→        value = """
  1042→        SELECT v.asset_id, COUNT(*) as vuln_count
  1043→        FROM vulnerability v
  1044→        WHERE v.id IN (
  1045→            SELECT id FROM (
  1046→                SELECT id, asset_id, import_timestamp,
  1047→                       DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY asset_id ORDER BY COALESCE(import_timestamp, '1970-01-01') DESC) as rnk
  1048→                FROM vulnerability
  1049→                WHERE asset_id IN :assetIds
  1050→            ) ranked
  1051→            WHERE rnk = 1
  1052→        )
  1053→        GROUP BY v.asset_id
  1054→        """,
  1055→        nativeQuery = true
  1056→    )
  1057→    fun countLatestVulnerabilitiesByAssetIds(assetIds: Set<Long>): List<Array<Any>>

  1059→    // Performance Optimization: SQL-level exception status filtering
  1060→    // Eliminates loading 358k rows into memory when filtering by overdue/excepted/ok status
  1061→
  1062→    /**
  1063→     * Find vulnerabilities with OVERDUE status (admin, no asset restriction)
  1064→     * OVERDUE = age > threshold AND no matching active exception
  1065→     */
  1066→    @io.micronaut.data.annotation.Query(
  1067→        value = """
  1068→        SELECT v.* FROM vulnerability v
  1069→        JOIN asset a ON v.asset_id = a.id
  1070→        WHERE v.scan_timestamp < :thresholdDate
  1071→        AND (:severity IS NULL OR v.cvss_severity = :severity)
  1072→        AND (:assetName IS NULL OR LOWER(a.name) LIKE LOWER(CONCAT('%', :assetName, '%')))
  1073→        AND (:productFilter IS NULL OR LOWER(v.vulnerable_product_versions) LIKE LOWER(CONCAT('%', :productFilter, '%')))
  1074→        AND (:domainFilter IS NULL OR a.ad_domain LIKE CONCAT('%', :domainFilter, '%'))
  1075→        AND (:cloudAccountIdFilter IS NULL OR a.cloud_account_id LIKE CONCAT('%', :cloudAccountIdFilter, '%'))
  1076→        AND NOT EXISTS (
  1077→            SELECT 1 FROM vulnerability_exception e
  1078→            WHERE (e.expiration_date IS NULL OR e.expiration_date > NOW())
  1079→            AND (
  1080→                (e.exception_type = 'IP' AND e.target_value = a.ip)
  1081→                OR (e.exception_type = 'PRODUCT' AND (e.target_value = v.vulnerability_id OR LOCATE(e.target_value, v.vulnerable_product_versions) > 0))
  1082→                OR (e.exception_type = 'ASSET' AND e.asset_id = a.id)
  1083→                OR (e.exception_type = 'CVE' AND FIND_IN_SET(v.vulnerability_id, e.target_value) > 0 AND (e.asset_id IS NULL OR e.asset_id = a.id))
  1084→            )
  1085→        )
  1086→        ORDER BY v.import_timestamp DESC, a.name ASC, v.vulnerability_id ASC
  1087→        LIMIT :pageSize OFFSET :pageOffset
  1088→        """,
  1089→        nativeQuery = true
  1090→    )

  1391→     * Feature: 054-products-overview
  1392→     *
  1393→     * @param assetIds Set of asset IDs the user has access to
  1394→     * @param limit Maximum number of products to return
  1395→     * @return List of TopProductRow with product name and vulnerability count
  1396→     */
  1397→    @io.micronaut.data.annotation.Query(
  1398→        value = """
  1399→        SELECT v.vulnerable_product_versions as product, COUNT(*) as vulnerabilityCount
  1400→        FROM vulnerability v
  1401→        WHERE v.asset_id IN (:assetIds)
  1402→        AND v.vulnerable_product_versions IS NOT NULL
  1403→        AND v.vulnerable_product_versions != ''
  1404→        GROUP BY v.vulnerable_product_versions
  1405→        ORDER BY vulnerabilityCount DESC
  1406→        LIMIT :limit
  1407→        """,
  1408→        nativeQuery = true
  1409→    )
  1410→    fun findTopProductsForAssets(assetIds: Set<Long>, limit: Int): List<TopProductRow>
  1411→
  1412→    // Memory Optimization - Feature 073
  1413→
  1414→    /**
  1415→     * Find duplicate vulnerability IDs using window function
  1416→     * Returns IDs of vulnerabilities that are duplicates (same asset, CVE, product)
  1417→     * Keeps only the most recent scan timestamp per unique combination
  1418→     *
  1419→     * Feature: 073-memory-optimization
  1420→     * Task: T008
  1421→     *
  1422→     * Uses batched processing to avoid loading all duplicates at once
  1423→     *
  1424→     * @param batchSize Maximum number of duplicate IDs to return per batch
  1425→     * @return List of vulnerability IDs to delete (duplicates)
  1426→     */
  1427→    @io.micronaut.data.annotation.Query(
  1428→        value = """
  1429→        SELECT id FROM (
  1430→            SELECT id, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
  1431→                PARTITION BY asset_id, vulnerability_id, vulnerable_product_versions
  1432→                ORDER BY scan_timestamp DESC
  1433→            ) as rn
  1434→            FROM vulnerability
  1435→        ) ranked
  1436→        WHERE rn > 1
  1437→        LIMIT :batchSize
  1438→        """,
  1439→        nativeQuery = true
  1440→    )
  1441→    fun findDuplicateIds(batchSize: Int): List<Long>
  1442→
  1443→    // SQL-level exception status filtering for non-admin users - Feature 073
  1444→    // These queries push access control AND exception status filtering to SQL
  1445→
  1446→    /**
  1447→     * Find vulnerabilities with OVERDUE status (non-admin, with access control)
  1448→     * OVERDUE = age > threshold AND no matching active exception AND in accessible assets
  1449→     *
  1450→     * Feature: 073-memory-optimization
  1451→     * Task: T009
  1452→     */
  1453→    @io.micronaut.data.annotation.Query(
  1454→        value = """
  1455→        SELECT v.* FROM vulnerability v
  1456→        JOIN asset a ON v.asset_id = a.id
  1457→        WHERE a.id IN (:accessibleAssetIds)
  1458→        AND v.scan_timestamp < :thresholdDate
  1459→        AND (:severity IS NULL OR v.cvss_severity = :severity)
  1460→        AND (:assetName IS NULL OR LOWER(a.name) LIKE LOWER(CONCAT('%', :assetName, '%')))
  1461→        AND (:productFilter IS NULL OR LOWER(v.vulnerable_product_versions) LIKE LOWER(CONCAT('%', :productFilter, '%')))
  1462→        AND (:domainFilter IS NULL OR a.ad_domain LIKE CONCAT('%', :domainFilter, '%'))
  1463→        AND (:cloudAccountIdFilter IS NULL OR a.cloud_account_id LIKE CONCAT('%', :cloudAccountIdFilter, '%'))
  1464→        AND NOT EXISTS (
  1465→            SELECT 1 FROM vulnerability_exception e
  1466→            WHERE (e.expiration_date IS NULL OR e.expiration_date > NOW())
  1467→            AND (
  1468→                (e.exception_type = 'IP' AND e.target_value = a.ip)
  1469→                OR (e.exception_type = 'PRODUCT' AND (e.target_value = v.vulnerability_id OR LOCATE(e.target_value, v.vulnerable_product_versions) > 0))
  1470→                OR (e.exception_type = 'ASSET' AND e.asset_id = a.id)
  1471→                OR (e.exception_type = 'CVE' AND FIND_IN_SET(v.vulnerability_id, e.target_value) > 0 AND (e.asset_id IS NULL OR e.asset_id = a.id))
  1472→            )
  1473→        )
  1474→        ORDER BY v.import_timestamp DESC, a.name ASC, v.vulnerability_id ASC
  1475→        LIMIT :pageSize OFFSET :pageOffset
  1476→        """,
  1477→        nativeQuery = true
  1478→    )

1→package com.secman.domain
     2→
     3→import com.fasterxml.jackson.annotation.JsonIgnore
     4→import io.micronaut.serde.annotation.Serdeable
     5→import jakarta.persistence.*
     6→import jakarta.validation.constraints.NotNull
     7→import java.time.LocalDateTime
     8→
     9→/**
    10→ * Vulnerability entity representing a security vulnerability discovered during a scan.
    11→ *
    12→ * Linked to an Asset entity via ManyToOne relationship.
    13→ * Feature 048: Uses transactional replace pattern - each import deletes existing vulnerabilities
    14→ * for an asset and inserts current ones, ensuring only the latest state is stored.
    15→ *
    16→ * Related to: Feature 003-i-want-to (Vulnerability Management System)
    17→ * Related to: Feature 048-prevent-duplicate-vulnerabilities
    18→ *
    19→ * @property id Unique identifier
    20→ * @property asset Asset this vulnerability affects (required)
    21→ * @property vulnerabilityId CVE or vulnerability identifier (e.g., "CVE-2016-2183")
    22→ * @property cvssSeverity CVSS severity level (e.g., "High", "Critical", "Medium", "Low", "Informational")
    23→ * @property vulnerableProductVersions Affected product/version information
    24→ * @property daysOpen Text representation of days open (e.g., "58 days") - display only
    25→ * @property scanTimestamp Discovery date (calculated from daysOpen for accurate overdue calculation)
    26→ * @property importTimestamp When this data was imported (same for all vulns in batch, for "current" query)
    27→ * @property patchPublicationDate When the vendor published the patch/fix (optional, from CrowdStrike API)
    28→ * @property createdAt When the record was first created in the database (auto-generated)
    29→ */
    30→@Entity
    31→@Table(
    32→    name = "vulnerability",
    33→    indexes = [
    34→        Index(name = "idx_vulnerability_asset", columnList = "asset_id"),
    35→        Index(name = "idx_vulnerability_asset_scan", columnList = "asset_id,scan_timestamp"),
    36→        Index(name = "idx_vulnerability_severity", columnList = "cvss_severity"),
    37→        // Query optimization indexes (Feature: Database Structure Optimization)
    38→        Index(name = "idx_vulnerability_cve", columnList = "vulnerability_id"),                    // CVE ID lookups
    39→        Index(name = "idx_vulnerability_asset_cve", columnList = "asset_id,vulnerability_id"),     // Duplicate detection
    40→        Index(name = "idx_vulnerability_product", columnList = "vulnerable_product_versions"),     // Product filtering
    41→        Index(name = "idx_vulnerability_scan_timestamp", columnList = "scan_timestamp"),           // Temporal queries
    42→        Index(name = "idx_vulnerability_import_ts", columnList = "asset_id,import_timestamp"),      // "Current" queries
    43→        Index(name = "idx_vulnerability_sort_order", columnList = "import_timestamp DESC, vulnerability_id")  // ORDER BY optimization for paginated queries
    44→    ]
    45→)
    46→@Serdeable
    47→data class Vulnerability(
    48→    @Id
    49→    @GeneratedValue(strategy = GenerationType.IDENTITY)
    50→    var id: Long? = null,
    51→
    52→    @ManyToOne(fetch = FetchType.LAZY)
    53→    @JoinColumn(name = "asset_id", nullable = false)
    54→    @JsonIgnore // Prevent lazy loading errors during JSON serialization
    55→    @NotNull
    56→    var asset: Asset,
    57→
    58→    @Column(name = "vulnerability_id", length = 255)
    59→    var vulnerabilityId: String? = null,
    60→
    61→    @Column(name = "cvss_severity", length = 50)
    62→    var cvssSeverity: String? = null,
    63→
    64→    @Column(name = "vulnerable_product_versions", length = 512)
    65→    var vulnerableProductVersions: String? = null,
    66→
    67→    @Column(name = "days_open", length = 50)
    68→    var daysOpen: String? = null,
    69→
    70→    @Column(name = "scan_timestamp", nullable = false)
    71→    @NotNull
    72→    var scanTimestamp: LocalDateTime,
    73→
    74→    @Column(name = "patch_publication_date")
    75→    var patchPublicationDate: LocalDateTime? = null,
    76→
    77→    @Column(name = "import_timestamp")
    78→    var importTimestamp: LocalDateTime? = null,
    79→
    80→    @Column(name = "created_at", updatable = false)
    81→    var createdAt: LocalDateTime? = null
    82→) {
    83→    @PrePersist
    84→    fun onCreate() {
    85→        val now = LocalDateTime.now()
    86→        createdAt = now
    87→        // Default importTimestamp to createdAt if not explicitly set
    88→        if (importTimestamp == null) {
    89→            importTimestamp = now
    90→        }
    91→    }
    92→
    93→    override fun toString(): String {
    94→        return "Vulnerability(id=$id, vulnerabilityId='$vulnerabilityId', " +
    95→                "cvssSeverity='$cvssSeverity', scanTimestamp=$scanTimestamp)"
    96→    }
    97→
    98→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean {
    99→        if (this === other) return true
   100→        if (other !is Vulnerability) return false
   101→        return id != null && id == other.id
   102→    }
   103→
   104→    override fun hashCode(): Int {
   105→        return id?.hashCode() ?: 0
   106→    }
   107→}
   108→

1→package com.secman.domain
     2→
     3→import com.fasterxml.jackson.annotation.JsonIgnore
     4→import io.micronaut.serde.annotation.Serdeable
     5→import jakarta.persistence.*
     6→import jakarta.validation.constraints.NotBlank
     7→import jakarta.validation.constraints.Size
     8→import java.time.LocalDateTime
     9→
    10→@Entity
    11→@Table(
    12→    name = "asset",
    13→    indexes = [
    14→        Index(name = "idx_asset_ip_numeric", columnList = "ip_numeric"),
    15→        // Query optimization indexes (Feature: Database Structure Optimization)
    16→        Index(name = "idx_asset_name", columnList = "name"),                      // Asset lookups, filters, sorting
    17→        Index(name = "idx_asset_cloud_account", columnList = "cloud_account_id"), // AWS account-based access control
    18→        Index(name = "idx_asset_ad_domain", columnList = "ad_domain"),            // AD domain filtering
    19→        Index(name = "idx_asset_type", columnList = "type"),                      // Asset type grouping/filtering
    20→        Index(name = "idx_asset_owner", columnList = "owner"),                    // Owner-based filtering
    21→        Index(name = "idx_asset_last_seen", columnList = "last_seen"),            // Outdated asset queries
    22→        Index(name = "idx_asset_manual_creator", columnList = "manual_creator_id"), // Access control by creator
    23→        Index(name = "idx_asset_scan_uploader", columnList = "scan_uploader_id")    // Access control by uploader
    24→    ]
    25→)
    26→@Serdeable
    27→data class Asset(
    28→    @Id
    29→    @GeneratedValue
    30→    var id: Long? = null,
    31→
    32→    @Column(nullable = false)
    33→    @NotBlank
    34→    @Size(max = 255)
    35→    var name: String,
    36→
    37→    @Column(nullable = false)
    38→    @NotBlank
    39→    var type: String,
    40→
    41→    @Column
    42→    var ip: String? = null,
    43→
    44→    /**
    45→     * Numeric representation of IP address for efficient range queries
    46→     * Feature: 020-i-want-to (IP Address Mapping)
    47→     * Computed from ip field in @PrePersist and @PreUpdate
    48→     * Example: "192.168.1.100" -> 3232235876
    49→     */
    50→    @Column(name = "ip_numeric", nullable = true)
    51→    var ipNumeric: Long? = null,
    52→
    53→    @Column(nullable = false)
    54→    @NotBlank
    55→    @Size(max = 255)
    56→    var owner: String,
    57→
    58→    @Column(length = 1024)
    59→    var description: String? = null,
    60→
    61→    @Column(name = "created_at", updatable = false)
    62→    var createdAt: LocalDateTime? = null,
    63→
    64→    @Column(name = "updated_at")
    65→    var updatedAt: LocalDateTime? = null,
    66→
    67→    /**
    68→     * Timestamp when asset was last seen in a scan
    69→     * Updated when new ScanResult is created for this asset
    70→     * Related to: Feature 002-implement-a-parsing (Nmap Scan Import)
    71→     */
    72→    @Column(name = "last_seen")
    73→    var lastSeen: LocalDateTime? = null,
    74→
    75→    /**
    76→     * Comma-separated group names this asset belongs to
    77→     * Example: "SVR-MS-DMZ,Production"
    78→     * Related to: Feature 003-i-want-to (Vulnerability Management System)
    79→     */
    80→    @Column(name = "groups", length = 512)
    81→    var groups: String? = null,
    82→
    83→    /**
    84→     * Cloud service account ID
    85→     * Related to: Feature 003-i-want-to (Vulnerability Management System)
    86→     */
    87→    @Column(name = "cloud_account_id", length = 255)
    88→    var cloudAccountId: String? = null,
    89→
    90→    /**
    91→     * Cloud service instance ID
    92→     * Related to: Feature 003-i-want-to (Vulnerability Management System)
    93→     */
    94→    @Column(name = "cloud_instance_id", length = 255)
    95→    var cloudInstanceId: String? = null,
    96→
    97→    /**
    98→     * Active Directory domain this asset belongs to
    99→     * Example: "MS.HOME"
   100→     * Related to: Feature 003-i-want-to (Vulnerability Management System)
   101→     */
   102→    @Column(name = "ad_domain", length = 255)
   103→    var adDomain: String? = null,
   104→
   105→    /**
   106→     * Operating system version
   107→     * Example: "Windows Server 2030", "Ubuntu 22.04"
   108→     * Related to: Feature 003-i-want-to (Vulnerability Management System)
   109→     */
   110→    @Column(name = "os_version", length = 255)
   111→    var osVersion: String? = null,
   112→
   113→    /**
   114→     * Explicit criticality override for this asset
   115→     * Feature 039: Asset and Workgroup Criticality Classification
   116→     * - nullable: null means inherit from workgroups
   117→     * - non-null: explicit override takes precedence over workgroup criticality
   118→     * - See effectiveCriticality for computed value
   119→     */
   120→    @Enumerated(EnumType.STRING)
   121→    @Column(name = "criticality", nullable = true, length = 20)
   122→    var criticality: Criticality? = null,
   123→
   124→    /**
   125→     * Many-to-many relationship with Workgroup
   126→     * Feature: 008-create-an-additional (Workgroup-Based Access Control)
   127→     * Feature: 073-memory-optimization (LAZY loading)
   128→     * Assets can belong to 0..n workgroups
   129→     *
   130→     * LAZY fetch: Workgroups loaded on-demand to reduce memory for list operations.
   131→     * Use AssetRepository.findByIdWithWorkgroups() when workgroups are needed.
   132→     * Feature flag MEMORY_LAZY_LOADING controls service-level behavior.
   133→     *
   134→     * @JsonIgnore prevents LazyInitializationException during JSON serialization.
   135→     * Workgroups should be accessed via service layer, not directly from API responses.
   136→     */
   137→    @JsonIgnore
   138→    @ManyToMany(fetch = FetchType.LAZY)
   139→    @JoinTable(
   140→        name = "asset_workgroups",
   141→        joinColumns = [JoinColumn(name = "asset_id")],
   142→        inverseJoinColumns = [JoinColumn(name = "workgroup_id")]
   143→    )
   144→    var workgroups: MutableSet<Workgroup> = mutableSetOf(),
   145→
   146→    /**
   147→     * Dual ownership tracking - manual creator
   148→     * Feature: 008-create-an-additional (Workgroup-Based Access Control)
   149→     * User who manually created this asset via UI
   150→     * Nullable: allows user deletion (FR-027)
   151→     * LAZY fetch: not needed for list operations
   152→     */
   153→    @ManyToOne(fetch = FetchType.LAZY)
   154→    @JoinColumn(name = "manual_creator_id", nullable = true)
   155→    var manualCreator: User? = null,
   156→
   157→    /**
   158→     * Dual ownership tracking - scan uploader
   159→     * Feature: 008-create-an-additional (Workgroup-Based Access Control)
   160→     * User who uploaded scan that discovered this asset
   161→     * Nullable: allows user deletion (FR-027)
   162→     * LAZY fetch: not needed for list operations
   163→     */
   164→    @ManyToOne(fetch = FetchType.LAZY)
   165→    @JoinColumn(name = "scan_uploader_id", nullable = true)
   166→    var scanUploader: User? = null,
   167→
   168→    /**
   169→     * Bidirectional relationship to ScanResult
   170→     * One asset can have multiple scan results over time (scan history)
   171→     * Foreign key is in scan_result table (asset_id)
   172→     * Related to: Feature 002-implement-a-parsing, Decision 4 (point-in-time snapshots)
   173→     *
   174→     * Note: @JsonIgnore prevents lazy loading errors during JSON serialization.
   175→     * Scan results should be loaded explicitly via port history endpoint.
   176→     */
   177→    @JsonIgnore
   178→    @OneToMany(mappedBy = "asset", cascade = [CascadeType.ALL], orphanRemoval = true, fetch = FetchType.LAZY)
   179→    var scanResults: MutableList<ScanResult> = mutableListOf(),
   180→
   181→    /**
   182→     * Bidirectional relationship to Vulnerability
   183→     * One asset can have multiple vulnerabilities discovered across different scans
   184→     * Foreign key is in vulnerability table (asset_id)
   185→     * Related to: Feature 003-i-want-to (Vulnerability Management System)
   186→     *
   187→     * Note: @JsonIgnore prevents lazy loading errors during JSON serialization.
   188→     * Vulnerabilities should be loaded explicitly via asset vulnerabilities endpoint.
   189→     */
   190→    @JsonIgnore
   191→    @OneToMany(mappedBy = "asset", fetch = FetchType.LAZY)
   192→    var vulnerabilities: MutableList<Vulnerability> = mutableListOf()
   193→) {
   194→    /**
   195→     * Computed effective criticality for this asset
   196→     * Feature 039: Asset and Workgroup Criticality Classification
   197→     *
   198→     * Calculation logic:
   199→     * 1. If asset has explicit criticality override -> return it
   200→     * 2. If asset belongs to 1+ workgroups -> return highest workgroup criticality (excluding N/A)
   201→     * 3. If all workgroups are N/A or asset has no workgroups -> default to MEDIUM
   202→     *
   203→     * Not persisted to database, computed on-demand
   204→     *
   205→     * @JsonIgnore: Prevents serialization from triggering LAZY workgroups load.
   206→     * Feature 073: Call this method only when workgroups are already loaded
   207→     * (e.g., via findByIdWithWorkgroups() or Hibernate.initialize()).
   208→     */
   209→    @JsonIgnore
   210→    fun getEffectiveCriticality(): Criticality {
   211→        return criticality ?: workgroups
   212→            .filter { it.criticality != Criticality.NA }  // Filter out N/A workgroups
   213→            .maxByOrNull { it.criticality }?.criticality ?: Criticality.MEDIUM
   214→    }
   215→
   216→    @PrePersist
   217→    fun onCreate() {
   218→        val now = LocalDateTime.now()
   219→        createdAt = now
   220→        updatedAt = now
   221→        computeIpNumeric()
   222→        normalizeDomain()
   223→    }
   224→
   225→    @PreUpdate
   226→    fun onUpdate() {
   227→        updatedAt = LocalDateTime.now()
   228→        computeIpNumeric()
   229→        normalizeDomain()
   230→    }
   231→
   232→    /**
   233→     * Normalize Active Directory domain to lowercase
   234→     * Feature: 043-crowdstrike-domain-import
   235→     * Ensures consistent storage and case-insensitive comparison
   236→     */
   237→    private fun normalizeDomain() {
   238→        adDomain = adDomain?.trim()?.lowercase()
   239→    }
   240→
   241→    /**
   242→     * Compute numeric representation of IP address
   243→     * Feature: 020-i-want-to (IP Address Mapping)
   244→     * Converts IPv4 address string to Long for efficient range queries
   245→     */
   246→    private fun computeIpNumeric() {
   247→        ipNumeric = if (ip != null) {
   248→            try {
   249→                ipToNumeric(ip!!)
   250→            } catch (e: Exception) {
   251→                null // Invalid IP format, skip numeric conversion
   252→            }
   253→        } else {
   254→            null
   255→        }
   256→    }
   257→
   258→    /**
   259→     * Convert IPv4 address string to numeric representation
   260→     * Example: "192.168.1.100" -> 3232235876
   261→     */
   262→    private fun ipToNumeric(ipAddress: String): Long {
   263→        val parts = ipAddress.split('.')
   264→        if (parts.size != 4) return 0L
   265→
   266→        var result = 0L
   267→        for (part in parts) {
   268→            val octet = part.toIntOrNull() ?: return 0L
   269→            if (octet < 0 || octet > 255) return 0L
   270→            result = (result shl 8) or octet.toLong()
   271→        }
   272→        return result
   273→    }
   274→
   275→    /**
   276→     * Add a scan result to this asset
   277→     * Maintains bidirectional relationship and updates lastSeen
   278→     */
   279→    fun addScanResult(result: ScanResult) {
   280→        scanResults.add(result)
   281→        result.asset = this
   282→        lastSeen = result.discoveredAt
   283→        updatedAt = LocalDateTime.now()
   284→    }
   285→
   286→    override fun toString(): String {
   287→        return "Asset(id=$id, name='$name', type='$type', owner='$owner', ip='$ip')"
   288→    }
   289→
   290→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean {
   291→        if (this === other) return true
   292→        if (other !is Asset) return false
   293→        return id != null && id == other.id
   294→    }
   295→
   296→    override fun hashCode(): Int {
   297→        return id?.hashCode() ?: 0
   298→    }
   299→}

I need to understand how the backend receives and processes CrowdStrike vulnerability imports, focusing on memory usage. Please explore:

1. Find the backend controller endpoint that receives CrowdStrike vulnerability data — look in `src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/controller/CrowdStrikeController.kt`
2. Find the service that processes the import — look at `src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/service/CrowdStrikeQueryService.kt` or similar service files
3. Look at how `importVulnerabilitiesForServer` works — the delete-then-insert pattern
4. Check the Vulnerability and Asset domain entities in `src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/domain/`
5. Look at `src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/config/MemoryOptimizationConfig.kt` for existing memory optimization settings

Focus on:
- How many entities are created in a single transaction
- Whether batch inserts are used (Hibernate batching)
- JPA entity graph — what gets loaded into the persistence context
- The size of the HTTP request body the backend receives
- Any existing memory optimization patterns (Feature 073)

Be very thorough — read complete files.

content.content:
1→package com.secman.config
     2→
     3→import io.micronaut.context.annotation.ConfigurationProperties
     4→import io.micronaut.serde.annotation.Serdeable
     5→
     6→/**
     7→ * Configuration for memory optimization features
     8→ * Feature: 073-memory-optimization
     9→ *
    10→ * Provides feature flags for stability-first rollback capability:
    11→ * - lazyLoadingEnabled: Toggle LAZY/EAGER entity loading
    12→ * - batchSize: Configurable batch size for large operations
    13→ * - streamingExportsEnabled: Toggle streaming vs buffered exports
    14→ *
    15→ * Environment variables:
    16→ * - MEMORY_LAZY_LOADING (default: true)
    17→ * - MEMORY_BATCH_SIZE (default: 1000)
    18→ * - MEMORY_STREAMING_EXPORTS (default: true)
    19→ */
    20→@ConfigurationProperties("secman.memory")
    21→@Serdeable
    22→data class MemoryOptimizationConfig(
    23→    /**
    24→     * Enable LAZY loading for entity relationships (default: true)
    25→     * When false, reverts to EAGER loading behavior for rollback
    26→     */
    27→    var lazyLoadingEnabled: Boolean = true,
    28→
    29→    /**
    30→     * Batch size for large data operations (default: 1000)
    31→     * Used for duplicate cleanup and bulk deletions
    32→     * Valid range: 100-10000
    33→     */
    34→    var batchSize: Int = 1000,
    35→
    36→    /**
    37→     * Enable streaming exports (default: true)
    38→     * When true, writes directly to Excel during fetch
    39→     * When false, accumulates all records first (original behavior)
    40→     */
    41→    var streamingExportsEnabled: Boolean = true
    42→)
    43→

2. Trace how vulnerabilities are fetched from CrowdStrike API — look at `src/cli/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/cli/service/` for CrowdStrike service classes.

content.content:
1→package com.secman.cli.service
     2→
     3→import com.secman.crowdstrike.client.CrowdStrikeApiClient
     4→import com.secman.crowdstrike.dto.CrowdStrikeQueryResponse
     5→import com.secman.crowdstrike.dto.FalconConfigDto
     6→import jakarta.inject.Singleton
     7→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
     8→
     9→/**
    10→ * Service for polling CrowdStrike API for HIGH and CRITICAL vulnerabilities
    11→ *
    12→ * Functionality:
    13→ * - Query all devices or specific hostnames
    14→ * - Filter for HIGH and CRITICAL severity
    15→ * - Store results via VulnerabilityStorageService
    16→ * - Handle pagination and rate limiting
    17→ *
    18→ * Related to: Feature 026-crowdstrike-polling-monitor
    19→ * Tasks: T5-T9
    20→ */
    21→@Singleton
    22→class CrowdStrikePollerService(
    23→    private val apiClient: CrowdStrikeApiClient,
    24→    private val storageService: VulnerabilityStorageService
    25→) {
    26→    private val log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(CrowdStrikePollerService::class.java)
    27→    
    28→    var backendUrl: String? = null
    29→    var dryRun: Boolean = false
    30→    var verbose: Boolean = false
    31→    
    32→    /**
    33→     * Poll all devices in CrowdStrike for HIGH/CRITICAL vulnerabilities
    34→     *
    35→     * Task: T5
    36→     *
    37→     * @param config CrowdStrike configuration
    38→     * @return PollResult with statistics
    39→     */
    40→    fun pollAllDevices(config: FalconConfigDto): PollResult {
    41→        log.info("Polling all devices for HIGH and CRITICAL vulnerabilities")
    42→        
    43→        // For now, we need to query specific hostnames since CrowdStrike API
    44→        // requires device ID or hostname. In production, you would:
    45→        // 1. First query /devices/queries/devices/v1 to get all device IDs
    46→        // 2. Then query each device for vulnerabilities
    47→        // This is a simplified implementation that requires hostnames
    48→        
    49→        log.warn("Poll all devices not fully implemented - requires hostname list")
    50→        return PollResult(
    51→            devicesQueried = 0,
    52→            totalVulnerabilities = 0,
    53→            stored = 0,
    54→            skipped = 0,
    55→            errors = emptyList()
    56→        )
    57→    }
    58→    
    59→    /**
    60→     * Poll specific hostnames for HIGH/CRITICAL vulnerabilities
    61→     *
    62→     * Task: T5-T6
    63→     *
    64→     * @param hostnames List of hostnames to query
    65→     * @param config CrowdStrike configuration
    66→     * @return PollResult with statistics
    67→     */
    68→    fun pollHostnames(hostnames: List<String>, config: FalconConfigDto): PollResult {
    69→        log.info("Polling {} hostnames for HIGH and CRITICAL vulnerabilities", hostnames.size)
    70→        
    71→        var devicesQueried = 0
    72→        var totalVulnerabilities = 0
    73→        var stored = 0
    74→        var skipped = 0
    75→        val errors = mutableListOf<String>()
    76→        
    77→        for (hostname in hostnames) {
    78→            try {
    79→                log.debug("Querying hostname: {}", hostname)
    80→                
    81→                // Query CrowdStrike API
    82→                val response = apiClient.queryAllVulnerabilities(hostname, config, limit = 5000)
    83→                devicesQueried++
    84→                
    85→                // Filter for HIGH and CRITICAL severity (Task: T6)
    86→                val highCriticalVulns = filterHighCritical(response)
    87→                totalVulnerabilities += highCriticalVulns.size
    88→                
    89→                if (highCriticalVulns.isEmpty()) {
    90→                    log.debug("No HIGH/CRITICAL vulnerabilities found for {}", hostname)
    91→                    continue
    92→                }
    93→                
    94→                log.info("Found {} HIGH/CRITICAL vulnerabilities for {}", highCriticalVulns.size, hostname)
    95→                
    96→                // Store vulnerabilities if not in dry-run mode
    97→                if (!dryRun) {
    98→                    val storeResult = storageService.storeVulnerabilities(
    99→                        hostname = hostname,
   100→                        vulnerabilities = highCriticalVulns,
   101→                        backendUrl = backendUrl
   102→                    )
   103→                    stored += storeResult.stored
   104→                    skipped += storeResult.skipped
   105→                } else {
   106→                    log.debug("Dry run mode - skipping storage")
   107→                }
   108→            } catch (e: Exception) {
   109→                val errorMsg = "Failed to poll $hostname: ${e.message}"
   110→                log.error(errorMsg, e)
   111→                errors.add(errorMsg)
   112→            }
   113→        }
   114→        
   115→        log.info("Poll complete: devices={}, vulnerabilities={}, stored={}, skipped={}, errors={}",
   116→            devicesQueried, totalVulnerabilities, stored, skipped, errors.size)
   117→        
   118→        return PollResult(
   119→            devicesQueried = devicesQueried,
   120→            totalVulnerabilities = totalVulnerabilities,
   121→            stored = stored,
   122→            skipped = skipped,
   123→            errors = errors
   124→        )
   125→    }
   126→    
   127→    /**
   128→     * Filter vulnerabilities for HIGH and CRITICAL severity
   129→     *
   130→     * Task: T6
   131→     *
   132→     * @param response CrowdStrike query response
   133→     * @return Filtered response with only HIGH/CRITICAL vulnerabilities
   134→     */
   135→    private fun filterHighCritical(response: CrowdStrikeQueryResponse): List<com.secman.crowdstrike.dto.CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityDto> {
   136→        return response.vulnerabilities.filter { vuln ->
   137→            vuln.severity.uppercase() in listOf("HIGH", "CRITICAL")
   138→        }
   139→    }
   140→}
   141→
   142→/**
   143→ * Result of a poll operation
   144→ */
   145→data class PollResult(
   146→    val devicesQueried: Int,
   147→    val totalVulnerabilities: Int,
   148→    val stored: Int,
   149→    val skipped: Int,
   150→    val errors: List<String>
   151→)
   152→

content.content:
1→package com.secman.cli.commands
     2→
     3→import com.secman.cli.config.ConfigLoader
     4→import com.secman.cli.export.ExportService
     5→import com.secman.crowdstrike.client.CrowdStrikeApiClient
     6→import com.secman.crowdstrike.dto.FalconConfigDto
     7→import com.secman.crowdstrike.exception.AuthenticationException
     8→import com.secman.crowdstrike.exception.CrowdStrikeException
     9→import com.secman.crowdstrike.exception.NotFoundException
    10→import com.secman.crowdstrike.exception.RateLimitException
    11→import com.secman.dto.CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityBatchDto
    12→import com.secman.dto.VulnerabilityDto
    13→import com.secman.service.CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityImportService
    14→import io.micronaut.context.ApplicationContext
    15→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
    16→import java.io.File
    17→
    18→/**
    19→ * Query command for CrowdStrike vulnerabilities
    20→ *
    21→ * Usage:
    22→ *   secman query --hostname <hostname> [options]
    23→ *
    24→ * Functionality:
    25→ * - Query CrowdStrike Falcon API for vulnerabilities
    26→ * - Filter by severity and product
    27→ * - Support pagination
    28→ * - Export to JSON or CSV
    29→ * - Optionally save to database (--save flag, direct DB access)
    30→ *
    31→ * Related to: Feature 023-create-in-the, 032-servers-query-import, 073-cli-direct-db
    32→ */
    33→class QueryCommand {
    34→    private val log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(QueryCommand::class.java)
    35→    private val configLoader = ConfigLoader()
    36→    private val exportService = ExportService()
    37→    private val appContext = ApplicationContext.run()
    38→    private val apiClient: CrowdStrikeApiClient = appContext.getBean(CrowdStrikeApiClient::class.java)
    39→    private val importService: CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityImportService = appContext.getBean(CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityImportService::class.java)
    40→
    41→    var hostname: String = ""
    42→    var outputPath: String? = null
    43→    var outputFile: String? = null
    44→    var format: String = "json"
    45→    var severity: String? = null
    46→    var product: String? = null
    47→    var limit: Int = 100
    48→    var clientId: String? = null
    49→    var clientSecret: String? = null
    50→    var verbose: Boolean = false
    51→    var save: Boolean = false
    52→
    53→    fun execute(): Int {
    54→        return try {
    55→            log.info("Querying vulnerabilities for hostname: {}", hostname)
    56→
    57→            // Validate input
    58→            if (hostname.isBlank()) {
    59→                System.err.println("Error: Hostname cannot be blank")
    60→                return 1
    61→            }
    62→
    63→            // Load or override configuration
    64→            val config = if (clientId != null && clientSecret != null) {
    65→                FalconConfigDto(
    66→                    clientId = clientId!!,
    67→                    clientSecret = clientSecret!!
    68→                )
    69→            } else {
    70→                configLoader.loadConfig()
    71→            }
    72→
    73→            log.info("Configuration loaded successfully")
    74→
    75→            // Query CrowdStrike API
    76→            log.info("Querying CrowdStrike API for hostname: {}", hostname)
    77→            System.out.println("Querying vulnerabilities for: $hostname")
    78→
    79→            val response = apiClient.queryAllVulnerabilities(hostname, config, limit)
    80→
    81→            // Filter by severity if specified (supports comma-separated values)
    82→            val filteredByServerity = if (severity != null) {
    83→                val severityLevels = severity!!.split(",").map { it.trim().lowercase() }
    84→                if (verbose) {
    85→                    System.out.println("Filtering by severity: ${severityLevels.joinToString(", ")}")
    86→                }
    87→                response.copy(
    88→                    vulnerabilities = response.vulnerabilities.filter {
    89→                        severityLevels.contains(it.severity.lowercase())
    90→                    }
    91→                )
    92→            } else {
    93→                response
    94→            }
    95→
    96→            // Filter by product if specified
    97→            val filteredByProduct = if (product != null) {
    98→                filteredByServerity.copy(
    99→                    vulnerabilities = filteredByServerity.vulnerabilities.filter {
   100→                        it.affectedProduct?.contains(product!!, ignoreCase = true) == true
   101→                    }
   102→                )
   103→            } else {
   104→                filteredByServerity
   105→            }
   106→
   107→            // Apply final filters
   108→            val finalResponse = filteredByProduct
   109→
   110→            System.out.println("Total vulnerabilities found: ${finalResponse.vulnerabilities.size}")
   111→
   112→            // Show severity breakdown if verbose
   113→            if (verbose && finalResponse.vulnerabilities.isNotEmpty()) {
   114→                val severityCount = finalResponse.vulnerabilities
   115→                    .groupBy { it.severity }
   116→                    .mapValues { it.value.size }
   117→                    .toList()
   118→                    .sortedByDescending { it.second }
   119→
   120→                System.out.println("\nSeverity breakdown:")
   121→                severityCount.forEach { (sev, count) ->
   122→                    System.out.println("  - $sev: $count")
   123→                }
   124→            }
   125→
   126→            // Save to database if --save flag is specified
   127→            if (save && finalResponse.vulnerabilities.isNotEmpty()) {
   128→                System.out.println("\nSaving to database...")
   129→
   130→                // Build a single batch DTO from the hostname + filtered vulnerabilities
   131→                val firstVuln = finalResponse.vulnerabilities.firstOrNull()
   132→                val batch = CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityBatchDto(
   133→                    hostname = hostname,
   134→                    groups = null,
   135→                    cloudAccountId = firstVuln?.cloudAccountId,
   136→                    cloudInstanceId = firstVuln?.cloudInstanceId,
   137→                    adDomain = firstVuln?.adDomain,
   138→                    osVersion = null,
   139→                    ip = firstVuln?.ip,
   140→                    vulnerabilities = finalResponse.vulnerabilities.map { vuln ->
   141→                        VulnerabilityDto(
   142→                            cveId = vuln.cveId ?: "",
   143→                            severity = vuln.severity,
   144→                            affectedProduct = vuln.affectedProduct,
   145→                            daysOpen = parseDaysOpenToInt(vuln.daysOpen),
   146→                            patchPublicationDate = vuln.patchPublicationDate
   147→                        )
   148→                    }
   149→                )
   150→
   151→                val result = importService.importServerVulnerabilities(listOf(batch), "CLI")
   152→
   153→                System.out.println("Save completed!")
   154→                System.out.println("  - Asset: ${if (result.serversCreated > 0) "CREATED" else "UPDATED"}")
   155→                System.out.println("  - Vulnerabilities imported: ${result.vulnerabilitiesImported}")
   156→                System.out.println("  - Vulnerabilities skipped: ${result.vulnerabilitiesSkipped}")
   157→
   158→                if (result.errors.isNotEmpty()) {
   159→                    System.err.println("  - Errors:")
   160→                    result.errors.forEach { error ->
   161→                        System.err.println("    - $error")
   162→                    }
   163→                }
   164→            } else if (save && finalResponse.vulnerabilities.isEmpty()) {
   165→                System.out.println("\nNo vulnerabilities to save")
   166→            }
   167→
   168→            // Export results if output file is specified
   169→            if (outputFile != null && finalResponse.vulnerabilities.isNotEmpty()) {
   170→                val outFile = File(outputFile!!)
   171→                val exported = when (format.lowercase()) {
   172→                    "csv" -> {
   173→                        exportService.exportToCsv(finalResponse, outFile)
   174→                    }
   175→                    else -> {
   176→                        exportService.exportToJson(finalResponse, outFile)
   177→                    }
   178→                }
   179→
   180→                if (exported) {
   181→                    System.out.println("Results exported to ${format.uppercase()}: ${outFile.absolutePath}")
   182→                } else {
   183→                    System.out.println("Export cancelled by user")
   184→                }
   185→            }
   186→
   187→            0
   188→        } catch (e: NotFoundException) {
   189→            System.err.println()
   190→            System.err.println("Error: Hostname '$hostname' not found in CrowdStrike")
   191→            System.err.println("   The hostname may not exist or may not be monitored by CrowdStrike Falcon.")
   192→            System.err.println("   Please verify the hostname is correct and the device is enrolled.")
   193→            1
   194→        } catch (e: AuthenticationException) {
   195→            System.err.println()
   196→            System.err.println("Error: CrowdStrike authentication failed")
   197→            System.err.println("   Please check your CrowdStrike API credentials.")
   198→            System.err.println("   Run 'secman config --show' to verify your configuration.")
   199→            1
   200→        } catch (e: RateLimitException) {
   201→            System.err.println()
   202→            System.err.println("Error: CrowdStrike API rate limit exceeded")
   203→            System.err.println("   Please wait a few minutes before trying again.")
   204→            1
   205→        } catch (e: CrowdStrikeException) {
   206→            System.err.println()
   207→            System.err.println("Error: CrowdStrike API error")
   208→            System.err.println("   ${e.message}")
   209→            if (verbose) {
   210→                e.printStackTrace()
   211→            }
   212→            1
   213→        } catch (e: IllegalStateException) {
   214→            System.err.println("Error: ${e.message}")
   215→            1
   216→        } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
   217→            System.err.println("Error: ${e.message}")
   218→            1
   219→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   220→            System.err.println("Error: ${e.message}")
   221→            if (verbose) {
   222→                e.printStackTrace()
   223→            }
   224→            1
   225→        }
   226→    }
   227→
   228→    /**
   229→     * Parse daysOpen string to integer value
   230→     *
   231→     * Handles formats: "526 days" -> 526, "1 day" -> 1, null -> 0
   232→     */
   233→    private fun parseDaysOpenToInt(daysOpen: String?): Int {
   234→        if (daysOpen.isNullOrBlank()) return 0
   235→        return daysOpen.split(" ").firstOrNull()?.toIntOrNull() ?: 0
   236→    }
   237→}

data.prompt:
I need to understand the full CrowdStrike vulnerability import pipeline from the CLI side, focusing on memory characteristics. Please trace the entire flow:

1. Find the CLI command that queries CrowdStrike and sends data to the backend. Look in `src/cli/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/cli/commands/` for CrowdStrike-related commands.
2. Trace how vulnerabilities are fetched from CrowdStrike API — look at `src/cli/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/cli/service/` for CrowdStrike service classes.
3. Find how the CLI batches/sends vulnerability data to the backend API endpoint.
4. Look at the DTOs used for the import (CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityBatchDto and related).

Focus on:
- How many vulnerabilities are held in memory at once
- Whether there's any streaming or batching
- What data structures accumulate in memory
- The HTTP payload size and structure
- Any pagination from the CrowdStrike API

Be very thorough — read complete files, not just snippets.

    16→
    17→/**
    18→ * Servers command for querying CrowdStrike server vulnerabilities with auto-import
    19→ *
    20→ * Usage:
    21→ *   secman query servers [options]
    22→ *
    23→ * Functionality:
    24→ * - Query CrowdStrike Falcon API for server vulnerabilities (device type: SERVER)
    25→ * - Filter by severity (HIGH, CRITICAL) and days open (>=30 by default)
    26→ * - Optionally filter by specific hostnames
    27→ * - Automatically import discovered servers as Asset records with vulnerabilities
    28→ * - Support dry-run mode (query without importing)
    29→ * - Support verbose logging
    30→ *
    31→ * Feature: 032-servers-query-import, 073-cli-direct-db
    32→ */
    33→class ServersCommand {
    34→    private val log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(ServersCommand::class.java)
    35→    private val configLoader = ConfigLoader()
    36→    private val appContext = ApplicationContext.run()
    37→    private val apiClient: CrowdStrikeApiClient = appContext.getBean(CrowdStrikeApiClient::class.java)

    39→

    44→    var minDaysOpen: Int = 30            // Minimum days open filter (FR-004)
    45→    var dryRun: Boolean = false          // Dry-run mode (FR-005)
    46→    var save: Boolean = false            // Save to database (direct access)
    47→    var verbose: Boolean = false         // Verbose logging (FR-009)
    48→    var clientId: String? = null         // CrowdStrike client ID (optional override)
    49→    var clientSecret: String? = null     // CrowdStrike client secret (optional override)
    50→    var limit: Int = 800                 // Page size for pagination
    51→    var lastSeenDays: Int = 0            // Only include devices seen within N days (0 = all devices)
    52→
    53→    /**
    54→     * Execute the servers query command
    55→     *
    56→     * @return Exit code (0 = success, 1 = error)
    57→     */
    58→    fun execute(): Int {
    59→        return try {
    60→            // Validate and parse device type (case-insensitive)
    61→            val parsedDeviceType = try {
    62→                DeviceType.fromString(deviceType)
    63→            } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {

    68→            // Always show which device type is being queried

    71→            if (verbose) {

    73→                    parsedDeviceType.name, severity, minDaysOpen, lastSeenDays, hostnames?.joinToString(",") ?: "ALL")
    74→            }
    75→
    76→            // Load or override configuration
    77→            val config = if (clientId != null && clientSecret != null) {
    78→                FalconConfigDto(
    79→                    clientId = clientId!!,
    80→                    clientSecret = clientSecret!!
    81→                )
    82→            } else {
    83→                configLoader.loadConfig()
    84→            }
    85→
    86→            if (verbose) {
    87→                log.info("Configuration loaded successfully")
    88→            }
    89→
    90→            // Query CrowdStrike API with filters
    91→            System.out.println("Querying CrowdStrike for ${parsedDeviceType.displayName()}...")
    92→            if (verbose) {
    93→                System.out.println("Filters: device type=${parsedDeviceType.name}, severity=$severity, min days open=$minDaysOpen, last seen days=${if (lastSeenDays > 0) lastSeenDays else "all"}")
    94→                if (hostnames != null) {
    95→                    System.out.println("Hostnames: ${hostnames!!.joinToString(", ")}")
    96→                }
    97→            }
    98→
    99→            // When --save is specified and no specific hostnames, use streaming mode
   100→            // to avoid holding all vulnerabilities in memory at once

   102→                System.out.println("Querying and importing in streaming mode...")
   103→
   104→                var totalServersProcessed = 0

   106→                var totalServersUpdated = 0
   107→                var totalVulnsImported = 0

   122→                    streamBatchNum++

   124→                    System.out.println("  Stream batch $streamBatchNum: ${batchVulns.size} vulns across ${byHostname.size} hosts")
   125→
   126→                    val batches = byHostname.map { (hostname, vulns) ->
   127→                        val firstVuln = vulns.firstOrNull()
   128→                        CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityBatchDto(

   149→                    totalServersProcessed += result.serversProcessed

   152→                    totalVulnsImported += result.vulnerabilitiesImported
   153→                    totalVulnsWithPatchDate += result.vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDate
   154→                    totalVulnsSkipped += result.vulnerabilitiesSkipped

   157→
   158→                if (totalVulns == 0) {

   166→                System.out.println("  - New $deviceLabel created: $totalServersCreated")
   167→                System.out.println("  - Existing $deviceLabel updated: $totalServersUpdated")
   168→                System.out.println("Vulnerabilities imported: $totalVulnsImported")

   171→
   172→                if (allErrors.isNotEmpty()) {

   174→                    allErrors.take(20).forEach { error ->

   179→                    }

   184→
   185→            // Non-streaming mode: query all at once (for dry-run, display, or hostname-specific queries)
   186→            val response = apiClient.queryServersWithFilters(
   187→                hostnames = hostnames,
   188→                deviceType = deviceType,
   189→                severity = severity,
   190→                minDaysOpen = minDaysOpen,
   191→                config = config,

   273→            if (result.errors.isNotEmpty()) 1 else 0

   321→            1
   322→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   323→            System.err.println("Error: [${e.javaClass.simpleName}] ${e.message}")
   324→            if (e.cause != null) {
   325→                System.err.println("  Caused by: [${e.cause!!.javaClass.simpleName}] ${e.cause!!.message}")
   326→            }
   327→            if (verbose) {
   328→                e.printStackTrace()
   329→            } else {
   330→                System.err.println("  (Run with --verbose for full stack trace)")
   331→            }
   332→            1
   333→        }
   334→    }
   335→
   336→    /**
   337→     * Parse daysOpen string to integer value
   338→     *
   339→     * Handles formats: "526 days" -> 526, "1 day" -> 1, null -> 0
   340→     */
   341→    private fun parseDaysOpenToInt(daysOpen: String?): Int {
   342→        if (daysOpen.isNullOrBlank()) return 0
   343→        return daysOpen.split(" ").firstOrNull()?.toIntOrNull() ?: 0
   344→    }
   345→}
   346→
