1→package com.secman.service
     2→
     3→import com.secman.config.VulnerabilitySettings
     4→import com.secman.domain.Asset
     5→import com.secman.domain.CrowdStrikeImportHistory
     6→import com.secman.domain.Vulnerability
     7→import com.secman.dto.CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityBatchDto
     8→import com.secman.dto.ImportStatisticsDto
     9→import com.secman.dto.CrowdStrikeImportStatusDto
    10→import com.secman.dto.VulnerabilityDto
    11→import com.secman.repository.AssetRepository
    12→import com.secman.repository.VulnerabilityRepository
    13→import com.secman.repository.CrowdStrikeImportHistoryRepository
    14→import jakarta.inject.Singleton
    15→import jakarta.persistence.EntityManager
    16→import jakarta.persistence.LockModeType
    17→import jakarta.transaction.Transactional
    18→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
    19→import java.time.LocalDateTime
    20→import java.time.temporal.ChronoUnit
    21→
    22→/**
    23→ * Service for importing CrowdStrike server vulnerabilities with automatic asset creation
    24→ *
    25→ * Provides functionality to:
    26→ * - Find or create Asset records from CrowdStrike server data
    27→ * - Import vulnerabilities with transactional replace pattern (delete old + insert new)
    28→ * - Track import statistics (servers created/updated, vulnerabilities imported/skipped)
    29→ * - Handle per-server transactions with rollback on failure
    30→ * - Preserve existing asset metadata (owner, description, workgroups) when reusing assets
    31→ *
    32→ * Feature: 032-servers-query-import
    33→ * Tasks: T019, T020, T021, T022
    34→ * Spec reference: FR-008, FR-010, FR-011, FR-012, FR-013, FR-015
    35→ */
    36→@Singleton
    37→open class CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityImportService(
    38→    private val assetRepository: AssetRepository,
    39→    private val vulnerabilityRepository: VulnerabilityRepository,
    40→    private val importHistoryRepository: CrowdStrikeImportHistoryRepository,
    41→    private val materializedViewRefreshService: MaterializedViewRefreshService,
    42→    private val vulnerabilitySettings: VulnerabilitySettings,
    43→    private val entityManager: EntityManager
    44→) {
    45→    private val log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityImportService::class.java)
    46→
    47→    /**
    48→     * Provider for self-reference to ensure @Transactional works on internal method calls.
    49→     * Using Provider<T> avoids circular dependency during bean construction.
    50→     * Feature 053: Fix AOP proxy bypass for transactional delete-insert pattern
    51→     */
    52→    @jakarta.inject.Inject
    53→    private lateinit var selfProvider: jakarta.inject.Provider<CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityImportService>
    54→
    55→    /**
    56→     * Import batch of server vulnerabilities from CrowdStrike
    57→     *
    58→     * Task: T022
    59→     * Spec reference: FR-008
    60→     *
    61→     * @param batches List of server vulnerability batches
    62→     * @param triggeredBy User/system that triggered the import (for audit trail)
    63→     * @param triggerRefresh Whether to trigger materialized view refresh after import.
    64→     *                       Set to false when importing multiple batches to avoid redundant refreshes.
    65→     *                       The caller should trigger a single refresh after all batches complete.
    66→     * @return ImportStatisticsDto with detailed statistics
    67→     */
    68→    fun importServerVulnerabilities(
    69→        batches: List<CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityBatchDto>,
    70→        triggeredBy: String? = null,
    71→        triggerRefresh: Boolean = true
    72→    ): ImportStatisticsDto {
    73→        log.info("Starting batch import of {} servers", batches.size)
    74→
    75→        var serversCreated = 0
    76→        var serversUpdated = 0
    77→        var vulnerabilitiesImported = 0
    78→        var vulnerabilitiesSkipped = 0
    79→        var vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDate = 0
    80→        val uniqueDomains = mutableSetOf<String>()  // Feature 043: Track unique domains
    81→        val errors = mutableListOf<String>()
    82→
    83→        for (batch in batches) {
    84→            try {
    85→                // Process each server in its own transaction (FR-010)
    86→                // Use selfProvider.get() to ensure @Transactional is applied via AOP proxy (Feature 053)
    87→                val result = selfProvider.get().importVulnerabilitiesForServer(batch)
    88→
    89→                if (result.assetCreated) serversCreated++
    90→                else serversUpdated++
    91→
    92→                vulnerabilitiesImported += result.vulnerabilitiesImported
    93→                vulnerabilitiesSkipped += result.vulnerabilitiesSkipped
    94→                vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDate += result.vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDate
    95→
    96→                // Track unique domains (Feature 043)
    97→                batch.adDomain?.let { domain ->
    98→                    if (domain.isNotBlank()) {
    99→                        uniqueDomains.add(domain.trim().uppercase())
   100→                        log.trace("Tracked domain: {}", domain.trim().uppercase())
   101→                    }
   102→                }
   103→
   104→                log.debug("Imported server '{}': created={}, imported={}, skipped={}, withPatchDate={}, domain={}",
   105→                    batch.hostname, result.assetCreated, result.vulnerabilitiesImported, result.vulnerabilitiesSkipped, result.vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDate, batch.adDomain)
   106→            } catch (e: Exception) {
   107→                val errorMsg = "Failed to import server '${batch.hostname}': ${e.message}"
   108→                log.error(errorMsg, e)
   109→                errors.add(errorMsg)
   110→            }
   111→        }
   112→
   113→        val statistics = ImportStatisticsDto(
   114→            serversProcessed = batches.size,
   115→            serversCreated = serversCreated,
   116→            serversUpdated = serversUpdated,
   117→            vulnerabilitiesImported = vulnerabilitiesImported,
   118→            vulnerabilitiesSkipped = vulnerabilitiesSkipped,
   119→            vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDate = vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDate,
   120→            uniqueDomainCount = uniqueDomains.size,  // Feature 043
   121→            discoveredDomains = uniqueDomains.sorted(),  // Feature 043
   122→            errors = errors
   123→        )
   124→
   125→        log.info("Batch import completed: processed={}, created={}, updated={}, imported={}, skipped={}, withPatchDate={}, uniqueDomains={}, errors={}",
   126→            batches.size, serversCreated, serversUpdated, vulnerabilitiesImported, vulnerabilitiesSkipped, vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDate, uniqueDomains.size, errors.size)
   127→
   128→        recordImportHistory(statistics, triggeredBy)
   129→
   130→        // Trigger async materialized view refresh after import (if requested)
   131→        // Feature: 034-outdated-assets, Task: T101
   132→        // Spec reference: FR-005 (async refresh)
   133→        // Note: When importing multiple batches, set triggerRefresh=false to avoid redundant refreshes.
   134→        // The caller should trigger a single refresh after all batches complete.
   135→        if (triggerRefresh && vulnerabilitiesImported > 0) {
   136→            log.info("Triggering materialized view refresh after importing {} vulnerabilities", vulnerabilitiesImported)
   137→            materializedViewRefreshService.triggerAsyncRefresh("CLI Import - $vulnerabilitiesImported vulnerabilities imported")
   138→        } else if (!triggerRefresh && vulnerabilitiesImported > 0) {
   139→            log.debug("Skipping materialized view refresh (triggerRefresh=false, caller will handle)")
   140→        }
   141→
   142→        return statistics
   143→    }
   144→
   145→    /**
   146→     * Persist import execution metadata for UI consumption.
   147→     */
   148→    private fun recordImportHistory(statistics: ImportStatisticsDto, triggeredBy: String?) {
   149→        try {
   150→            val history = CrowdStrikeImportHistory(
   151→                importedAt = LocalDateTime.now(),
   152→                importedBy = triggeredBy,
   153→                serversProcessed = statistics.serversProcessed,
   154→                serversCreated = statistics.serversCreated,
   155→                serversUpdated = statistics.serversUpdated,
   156→                vulnerabilitiesImported = statistics.vulnerabilitiesImported,
   157→                vulnerabilitiesSkipped = statistics.vulnerabilitiesSkipped,
   158→                vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDate = statistics.vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDate,
   159→                errorCount = statistics.errors.size
   160→            )
   161→            importHistoryRepository.save(history)
   162→            log.info("Recorded CrowdStrike import history entry: {}", history)
   163→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   164→            log.error("Failed to record CrowdStrike import history", e)
   165→        }
   166→    }
   167→
   168→    fun getLatestImportStatus(): CrowdStrikeImportStatusDto? {
   169→        return importHistoryRepository.findLatest()
   170→            ?.let { CrowdStrikeImportStatusDto.fromEntity(it) }
   171→    }
   172→
   173→    /**
   174→     * Import vulnerabilities for a single server with transactional replace pattern
   175→     *
   176→     * **Duplicate Prevention Strategy (Feature 048):**
   177→     * This method prevents duplicate vulnerability entries by implementing a transactional replace pattern:
   178→     * 1. DELETE all existing vulnerabilities for the asset
   179→     * 2. INSERT new vulnerabilities from the import batch
   180→     * 3. All operations wrapped in @Transactional boundary
   181→     *
   182→     * This approach ensures:
   183→     * - **Idempotency**: Running the same import multiple times produces identical results
   184→     * - **No duplicates**: Each (asset, CVE) combination exists exactly once
   185→     * - **Atomicity**: Either all vulnerabilities are replaced, or none are (on failure)
   186→     * - **Remediation tracking**: Removed CVEs indicate vulnerabilities have been patched
   187→     *
   188→     * The transactional replace pattern is preferred over upsert because:
   189→     * - Simpler logic: No need to detect which records to update vs insert
   190→     * - Accurate remediation: Missing CVEs in new import = vulnerability fixed
   191→     * - Better performance: Bulk delete + bulk insert faster than individual upserts
   192→     * - Cleaner state: No orphaned records from complex merge logic
   193→     *
   194→     * **Important Implementation Detail:**
   195→     * The Asset.vulnerabilities relationship MUST NOT use `cascade = [CascadeType.ALL]` or `orphanRemoval = true`.
   196→     * Manual delete-insert operations conflict with JPA cascade behavior, causing newly inserted
   197→     * vulnerabilities to be cascade-deleted when the transaction commits. This service performs
   198→     * explicit vulnerability deletion via `vulnerabilityRepository.deleteByAssetId()` before insertion.
   199→     *
   200→     * Tasks: T021, T024, T025, T026
   201→     * Spec reference: FR-010, FR-011, Feature 048
   202→     *
   203→     * @param batch Server vulnerability batch containing hostname and vulnerabilities
   204→     * @return ServerImportResult with statistics (vulnerabilities imported/skipped, asset created/updated)
   205→     */
   206→    @Transactional
   207→    open fun importVulnerabilitiesForServer(batch: CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityBatchDto): ServerImportResult {
   208→        log.debug("Importing vulnerabilities for server: {}", batch.hostname)
   209→
   210→        // Feature 053: Duplicate Asset Cleanup
   211→        // Instead of strict exact match, we look for potential duplicates (FQDN vs Shortname)
   212→        val shortName = batch.hostname.substringBefore(".")
   213→        val candidates = assetRepository.findPotentialDuplicates(shortName)
   214→
   215→        val (asset, isNewAsset) = if (candidates.isNotEmpty()) {
   216→            // Pick best match: exact match first, or the one with content, or just the first one
   217→            val bestMatch = candidates.find { it.name.equals(batch.hostname, ignoreCase = true) } 
   218→                           ?: candidates.first()
   219→            
   220→            // Clean up: We will delete vulnerabilities for ALL candidates to avoid inflation
   221→            // The "best match" will get new vulnerabilities populated below
   222→            
   223→            Pair(updateAsset(bestMatch, batch), false)
   224→        } else {
   225→            Pair(createNewAsset(batch), true)
   226→        }
   227→
   228→        // Delete existing vulnerabilities for this asset AND any duplicates
   229→        // This ensures that "stale" duplicate assets don't contribute to inflated counts
   230→        val assetIdsToClean = if (candidates.isNotEmpty()) {
   231→            candidates.mapNotNull { it.id }
   232→        } else {
   233→            listOf(asset.id!!)
   234→        }
   235→
   236→        var deletedCount = 0
   237→        for (id in assetIdsToClean) {
   238→            // Lock asset to prevent concurrent import races
   239→            entityManager.find(Asset::class.java, id, LockModeType.PESSIMISTIC_WRITE)
   240→            val count = vulnerabilityRepository.deleteByAssetId(id)
   241→            deletedCount += count
   242→        }
   243→        
   244→        if (deletedCount > 0) {
   245→            log.debug("Deleted {} existing vulnerabilities for asset '{}' (and {} duplicates)", 
   246→                deletedCount, asset.name, assetIdsToClean.size - 1)
   247→        }
   248→
   249→        // Filter vulnerabilities: require CVE ID and optionally require patch publication date
   250→        // Combined into a single pass to reduce intermediate list allocations (memory optimization)
   251→        val requirePatchDate = vulnerabilitySettings.requirePatchPublicationDate
   252→        var vulnsWithCve = batch.vulnerabilities.filter { vuln ->
   253→            !vuln.cveId.isNullOrBlank() && (!requirePatchDate || vuln.patchPublicationDate != null)
   254→        }
   255→        val skippedCount = batch.vulnerabilities.size - vulnsWithCve.size
   256→
   257→        if (skippedCount > 0) {
   258→            log.debug("Skipped {} vulnerabilities (no CVE ID{}) for asset '{}'",
   259→                skippedCount, if (requirePatchDate) " or no patch publication date" else "", asset.name)
   260→        }
   261→
   262→        // Feature 054: Deduplicate by (CVE ID, affected product) before inserting
   263→        // CrowdStrike API may return true duplicates from overlapping pagination batches
   264→        // We keep the one with the highest daysOpen value (oldest occurrence)
   265→        // Different products for the same CVE are preserved as separate entries
   266→        val beforeDedup = vulnsWithCve.size
   267→        vulnsWithCve = vulnsWithCve
   268→            .groupBy { Pair(it.cveId, it.affectedProduct) }
   269→            .map { (_, duplicates) -> duplicates.maxByOrNull { it.daysOpen } ?: duplicates.first() }
   270→
   271→        val dedupCount = beforeDedup - vulnsWithCve.size
   272→        if (dedupCount > 0) {
   273→            log.info("Deduplicated {} duplicate (CVE, product) entries for asset '{}' ({} -> {} unique)",
   274→                dedupCount, asset.name, beforeDedup, vulnsWithCve.size)
   275→        }
   276→
   277→        // Create new vulnerability records (T021)
   278→        // importTimestamp is the same for all vulnerabilities in this batch (used for "current" query)
   279→        val importTimestamp = LocalDateTime.now()
   280→        var vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDateCount = 0
   281→        val vulnerabilities = vulnsWithCve.map { vulnDto ->
   282→            // Track vulnerabilities with patch publication date
   283→            if (vulnDto.patchPublicationDate != null) {
   284→                vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDateCount++
   285→            }
   286→
   287→            // Calculate scan timestamp and days open
   288→            // scanTimestamp represents when the vulnerability was discovered (for accurate overdue calculation)
   289→            // importTimestamp represents when this data was imported (for "current" query grouping)
   290→            val (scanTimestamp, daysOpenText) = if (vulnerabilitySettings.usePatchPublicationDate && vulnDto.patchPublicationDate != null) {
   291→                // Use patch publication date as the reference timestamp (Feature 041)
   292→                val patchDate = vulnDto.patchPublicationDate
   293→                val daysFromPatch = ChronoUnit.DAYS.between(patchDate, importTimestamp).toInt()
   294→                val daysText = if (daysFromPatch == 1) "1 day" else "$daysFromPatch days"
   295→
   296→                log.trace("Using patch publication date for CVE {}: patch_date={}, days_from_patch={}",
   297→                    vulnDto.cveId, patchDate, daysFromPatch)
   298→
   299→                Pair(patchDate, daysText)  // Use patch date as scanTimestamp
   300→            } else {
   301→                // Calculate discovery timestamp from days_open (working backwards from now)
   302→                val discoveryTimestamp = importTimestamp.minusDays(vulnDto.daysOpen.toLong())
   303→                val daysText = "${vulnDto.daysOpen} days"
   304→                Pair(discoveryTimestamp, daysText)  // Use calculated discovery date as scanTimestamp
   305→            }
   306→
   307→            Vulnerability(
   308→                asset = asset,
   309→                vulnerabilityId = vulnDto.cveId,
   310→                cvssSeverity = vulnDto.severity,
   311→                vulnerableProductVersions = vulnDto.affectedProduct,
   312→                daysOpen = daysOpenText,
   313→                scanTimestamp = scanTimestamp,
   314→                importTimestamp = importTimestamp,
   315→                patchPublicationDate = vulnDto.patchPublicationDate
   316→            )
   317→        }
   318→
   319→        // Save vulnerabilities in chunks to prevent Hibernate first-level cache from growing unbounded
   320→        // For large imports (e.g., 2000+ vulns per server), chunking with flush/clear reduces memory pressure
   321→        if (vulnerabilities.isNotEmpty()) {
   322→            val chunkSize = 500
   323→            if (vulnerabilities.size <= chunkSize) {
   324→                vulnerabilityRepository.saveAll(vulnerabilities)
   325→            } else {
   326→                vulnerabilities.chunked(chunkSize).forEachIndexed { index, chunk ->
   327→                    vulnerabilityRepository.saveAll(chunk)
   328→                    entityManager.flush()
   329→                    entityManager.clear()
   330→                    // Re-attach the asset entity after clear() so subsequent chunks can reference it
   331→                    entityManager.merge(asset)
   332→                    log.trace("Saved vulnerability chunk {}/{} for asset '{}'",
   333→                        index + 1, (vulnerabilities.size + chunkSize - 1) / chunkSize, asset.name)
   334→                }
   335→            }
   336→            log.debug("Created {} new vulnerabilities for asset '{}' ({} with patch publication date)",
   337→                vulnerabilities.size, asset.name, vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDateCount)
   338→        }
   339→
   340→        return ServerImportResult(
   341→            assetCreated = isNewAsset,
   342→            vulnerabilitiesImported = vulnerabilities.size,
   343→            vulnerabilitiesSkipped = skippedCount,
   344→            vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDate = vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDateCount
   345→        )
   346→    }
   347→
   348→    private fun createNewAsset(batch: CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityBatchDto): Asset {
   349→        val newAsset = Asset(
   350→            name = batch.hostname,
   351→            type = "SERVER",  // FR-002
   352→            owner = "CrowdStrike Import",  // FR-012
   353→            description = null,
   354→            ip = batch.ip?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() },
   355→            groups = batch.groups?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() },
   356→            cloudAccountId = batch.cloudAccountId?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() },
   357→            cloudInstanceId = batch.cloudInstanceId?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() },
   358→            adDomain = batch.adDomain?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() },
   359→            osVersion = batch.osVersion?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() },
   360→            lastSeen = LocalDateTime.now(),
   361→            manualCreator = null,  // Not manually created
   362→            scanUploader = null    // Not from scan upload
   363→        )
   364→
   365→        val created = assetRepository.save(newAsset)
   366→        log.debug("Created new asset: {} (id={})", batch.hostname, created.id)
   367→        return created
   368→    }
   369→
   370→    private fun updateAsset(existing: Asset, batch: CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityBatchDto): Asset {
   371→        // Smart update: Only modify fields that have changed (Feature 043: US3)
   372→        var hasChanges = false
   373→        val changedFields = mutableListOf<String>()
   374→
   375→        // Compare and update IP
   376→        val newIp = batch.ip?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() }
   377→        if (newIp != null && newIp != existing.ip) {
   378→            log.debug("IP changed for {}: {} -> {}", batch.hostname, existing.ip, newIp)
   379→            existing.ip = newIp
   380→            hasChanges = true
   381→            changedFields.add("ip")
   382→        }
   383→
   384→        // Compare and update groups
   385→        val newGroups = batch.groups?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() }
   386→        if (newGroups != null && newGroups != existing.groups) {
   387→            log.debug("Groups changed for {}: {} -> {}", batch.hostname, existing.groups, newGroups)
   388→            existing.groups = newGroups
   389→            hasChanges = true
   390→            changedFields.add("groups")
   391→        }
   392→
   393→        // Compare and update cloud account ID
   394→        val newCloudAccountId = batch.cloudAccountId?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() }
   395→        if (newCloudAccountId != null && newCloudAccountId != existing.cloudAccountId) {
   396→            log.debug("Cloud account ID changed for {}: {} -> {}", batch.hostname, existing.cloudAccountId, newCloudAccountId)
   397→            existing.cloudAccountId = newCloudAccountId
   398→            hasChanges = true
   399→            changedFields.add("cloudAccountId")
   400→        }
   401→
   402→        // Compare and update cloud instance ID
   403→        val newCloudInstanceId = batch.cloudInstanceId?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() }
   404→        if (newCloudInstanceId != null && newCloudInstanceId != existing.cloudInstanceId) {
   405→            log.debug("Cloud instance ID changed for {}: {} -> {}", batch.hostname, existing.cloudInstanceId, newCloudInstanceId)
   406→            existing.cloudInstanceId = newCloudInstanceId
   407→            hasChanges = true
   408→            changedFields.add("cloudInstanceId")
   409→        }
   410→
   411→        // Compare and update AD domain (case-insensitive, normalized to lowercase by @PreUpdate)
   412→        val newAdDomain = batch.adDomain?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() }
   413→        if (newAdDomain != null && newAdDomain.lowercase() != existing.adDomain?.lowercase()) {
   414→            log.debug("AD domain changed for {}: {} -> {}", batch.hostname, existing.adDomain, newAdDomain)
   415→            existing.adDomain = newAdDomain
   416→            hasChanges = true
   417→            changedFields.add("adDomain")
   418→        }
   419→
   420→        // Compare and update OS version
   421→        val newOsVersion = batch.osVersion?.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() }
   422→        if (newOsVersion != null && newOsVersion != existing.osVersion) {
   423→            log.debug("OS version changed for {}: {} -> {}", batch.hostname, existing.osVersion, newOsVersion)
   424→            existing.osVersion = newOsVersion
   425→            hasChanges = true
   426→            changedFields.add("osVersion")
   427→        }
   428→
   429→        // Always update lastSeen
   430→        existing.lastSeen = LocalDateTime.now()
   431→
   432→        // Only write to database if fields changed
   433→        return if (hasChanges) {
   434→            existing.updatedAt = LocalDateTime.now()
   435→            assetRepository.update(existing)
   436→            log.info("Updated asset '{}' (id={}): changed fields: {}", batch.hostname, existing.id, changedFields.joinToString(", "))
   437→            existing
   438→        } else {
   439→            log.debug("No changes for asset '{}' (id={}), skipping database write", batch.hostname, existing.id)
   440→            existing
   441→        }
   442→    }
   443→}
   444→
   445→/**
   446→ * Result of importing vulnerabilities for a single server
   447→ */
   448→data class ServerImportResult(
   449→    val assetCreated: Boolean,
   450→    val vulnerabilitiesImported: Int,
   451→    val vulnerabilitiesSkipped: Int,
   452→    val vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDate: Int
   453→)

1→package com.secman.dto
     2→
     3→import io.micronaut.serde.annotation.Serdeable
     4→import jakarta.validation.constraints.NotBlank
     5→import jakarta.validation.constraints.NotNull
     6→import java.time.LocalDateTime
     7→
     8→/**
     9→ * DTO representing a vulnerability from CrowdStrike Falcon API
    10→ *
    11→ * Related to: Feature 015-we-have-currently (CrowdStrike System Vulnerability Lookup)
    12→ */
    13→@Serdeable
    14→data class CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityDto(
    15→    /**
    16→     * Unique vulnerability ID from CrowdStrike
    17→     */
    18→    @field:NotBlank
    19→    val id: String,
    20→
    21→    /**
    22→     * System hostname
    23→     */
    24→    @field:NotBlank
    25→    val hostname: String,
    26→
    27→    /**
    28→     * System IP address (nullable)
    29→     */
    30→    val ip: String?,
    31→
    32→    /**
    33→     * CVE identifier (nullable)
    34→     */
    35→    val cveId: String?,
    36→
    37→    /**
    38→     * CVSS severity level (Critical, High, Medium, Low, Informational)
    39→     */
    40→    @field:NotBlank
    41→    val severity: String,
    42→
    43→    /**
    44→     * Numeric CVSS score (0.0-10.0, nullable)
    45→     */
    46→    val cvssScore: Double?,
    47→
    48→    /**
    49→     * Vulnerable product and version (nullable)
    50→     */
    51→    val affectedProduct: String?,
    52→
    53→    /**
    54→     * Days since detection (calculated, e.g., "15 days")
    55→     */
    56→    val daysOpen: String?,
    57→
    58→    /**
    59→     * Detection timestamp (ISO 8601)
    60→     */
    61→    @field:NotNull
    62→    val detectedAt: LocalDateTime,
    63→
    64→    /**
    65→     * Patch publication date from vendor (nullable, from CrowdStrike API)
    66→     */
    67→    val patchPublicationDate: LocalDateTime? = null,
    68→
    69→    /**
    70→     * Vulnerability status (always "open" for this query)
    71→     */
    72→    @field:NotBlank
    73→    val status: String,
    74→
    75→    /**
    76→     * Whether this vulnerability matches an active exception
    77→     */
    78→    @field:NotNull
    79→    val hasException: Boolean,
    80→
    81→    /**
    82→     * Reason for exception if hasException is true (nullable)
    83→     */
    84→    val exceptionReason: String? = null
    85→)

1→package com.secman.dto
     2→
     3→import io.micronaut.serde.annotation.Serdeable
     4→import jakarta.validation.Valid
     5→import jakarta.validation.constraints.NotBlank
     6→import jakarta.validation.constraints.NotEmpty

     8→
     9→/**
    10→ * Request DTO for saving CrowdStrike vulnerabilities to database
    11→ *
    12→ * Related to:
    13→ * - Feature 015-we-have-currently (CrowdStrike System Vulnerability Lookup)
    14→ * - Feature 041-falcon-instance-lookup (AWS Instance ID Support)
    15→ */
    16→@Serdeable
    17→data class CrowdStrikeSaveRequest(
    18→    /**
    19→     * System hostname (used for asset matching/creation)
    20→     */
    21→    @field:NotBlank(message = "Hostname is required")
    22→    @field:Size(min = 1, max = 255, message = "Hostname must be between 1 and 255 characters")
    23→    val hostname: String,
    24→
    25→    /**
    26→     * List of vulnerabilities to save (at least one required)
    27→     */

    34→     * Feature: 041-falcon-instance-lookup

    36→     *
    37→     * When present, this value will be stored in the asset's cloudInstanceId field
    38→     * for asset enrichment and tracking purposes.
    39→     */
    40→    @field:Size(max = 19, message = "Instance ID must not exceed 19 characters")
    41→    val instanceId: String? = null

1→package com.secman.dto
     2→
     3→import io.micronaut.serde.annotation.Serdeable
     4→import jakarta.validation.Valid
     5→import jakarta.validation.constraints.NotBlank
     6→import jakarta.validation.constraints.NotNull
     7→import jakarta.validation.constraints.Size
     8→
     9→/**
    10→ * Request DTO for /api/crowdstrike/vulnerabilities/save endpoint
    11→ *
    12→ * Feature: 032-servers-query-import
    13→ * Spec reference: FR-015a
    14→ *
    15→ * Contains server metadata and its associated vulnerabilities for batch import.
    16→ * Supports transaction-per-server logic with atomic delete+insert operations.
    17→ *
    18→ * @property hostname Server hostname (used as Asset.name, unique identifier)
    19→ * @property groups Comma-separated group names from CrowdStrike
    20→ * @property cloudAccountId AWS/cloud account ID
    21→ * @property cloudInstanceId Cloud instance identifier
    22→ * @property adDomain Active Directory domain
    23→ * @property osVersion Operating system version
    24→ * @property ip IP address
    25→ * @property vulnerabilities List of vulnerabilities for this server
    26→ */
    27→@Serdeable
    28→data class CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityBatchDto(
    29→    @field:NotBlank
    30→    @field:Size(min = 1, max = 255)
    31→    val hostname: String,
    32→
    33→    @field:Size(max = 512)
    34→    val groups: String?,
    35→
    36→    @field:Size(max = 255)
    37→    val cloudAccountId: String?,
    38→
    39→    @field:Size(max = 255)
    40→    val cloudInstanceId: String?,
    41→
    42→    @field:Size(max = 255)
    43→    val adDomain: String?,
    44→
    45→    @field:Size(max = 255)
    46→    val osVersion: String?,
    47→
    48→    @field:Size(max = 255)
    49→    val ip: String?,
    50→
    51→    @field:NotNull
    52→    @field:Valid
    53→    @field:Size(max = 5000)
    54→    val vulnerabilities: List<VulnerabilityDto>
    55→)
    56→

1→micronaut:
     2→  application:
     3→    name: secman-backend-ng
     4→  executors:
     5→    email:
     6→      type: scheduled
     7→      core-pool-size: 2
     8→      maximum-pool-size: 5
     9→  server:
    10→    port: 8080
    11→    max-request-size: 100MB  # Increased from 20MB for large vulnerability batch imports
    12→    multipart:
    13→      max-file-size: 100MB   # Increased from 20MB for large file uploads
    14→      disk: true
    15→    cors:
    16→      enabled: true
    17→      configurations:
    18→        web:
    19→          allowed-origins:
    20→            - "http://localhost:4321"
    21→            - "http://localhost:3000"
    22→          allowed-methods:
    23→            - GET
    24→            - POST
    25→            - PUT
    26→            - DELETE
    27→            - OPTIONS
    28→          allowed-headers:
    29→            - Content-Type
    30→            - Authorization
    31→            - Accept
    32→            - Origin
    33→            - Csrf-Token
    34→          exposed-headers:
    35→            - Authorization
    36→          allow-credentials: true
    37→          max-age: 3600
    38→        # MCP Streamable HTTP Transport CORS configuration
    39→        # Allows direct connections from Claude Desktop and other MCP clients
    40→        mcp:
    41→          allowed-origins-regex:
    42→            - ".*"  # MCP clients can come from anywhere (non-browser)
    43→          allowed-methods:
    44→            - GET
    45→            - POST
    46→            - DELETE
    47→            - OPTIONS
    48→          allowed-headers:
    49→            - Content-Type
    50→            - Accept
    51→            - Mcp-Session-Id
    52→            - X-MCP-API-Key
    53→            - X-MCP-User-Email
    54→            - Origin
    55→          exposed-headers:
    56→            - Mcp-Session-Id
    57→          allow-credentials: false
    58→          max-age: 3600
    59→  security:
    60→    authentication: bearer
    61→    token:
    62→      jwt:
    63→        signatures:
    64→          secret:
    65→            generator:
    66→              secret: ${JWT_SECRET:pleasechangethissecrectokeyproductionforsecurityreasonsmustbe256bits}
    67→        generator:
    68→          access-token:
    69→            expiration: 28800  # 8 hours - extended for workday coverage
    70→        claims-validators:
    71→          issuer: secman-backend-ng
    72→    oauth2:

   179→  mcp:

   184→        # Time window in minutes for threshold calculation
   185→        window-minutes: ${MCP_DELEGATION_ALERT_WINDOW:5}
   186→  # OAuth robustness configuration
   187→  oauth:

   197→    token-exchange:

1→package com.secman.dto
     2→
     3→import io.micronaut.serde.annotation.Serdeable
     4→import jakarta.validation.Valid
     5→import jakarta.validation.constraints.NotBlank
     6→import jakarta.validation.constraints.NotNull
     7→import jakarta.validation.constraints.Size
     8→
     9→/**
    10→ * Request DTO for /api/crowdstrike/vulnerabilities/save endpoint
    11→ *
    12→ * Feature: 032-servers-query-import
    13→ * Spec reference: FR-015a
    14→ *
    15→ * Contains server metadata and its associated vulnerabilities for batch import.
    16→ * Supports transaction-per-server logic with atomic delete+insert operations.
    17→ *
    18→ * @property hostname Server hostname (used as Asset.name, unique identifier)
    19→ * @property groups Comma-separated group names from CrowdStrike
    20→ * @property cloudAccountId AWS/cloud account ID
    21→ * @property cloudInstanceId Cloud instance identifier
    22→ * @property adDomain Active Directory domain
    23→ * @property osVersion Operating system version
    24→ * @property ip IP address
    25→ * @property vulnerabilities List of vulnerabilities for this server
    26→ */
    27→@Serdeable
    28→data class CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityBatchDto(
    29→    @field:NotBlank
    30→    @field:Size(min = 1, max = 255)
    31→    val hostname: String,
    32→
    33→    @field:Size(max = 512)
    34→    val groups: String?,
    35→
    36→    @field:Size(max = 255)
    37→    val cloudAccountId: String?,
    38→
    39→    @field:Size(max = 255)
    40→    val cloudInstanceId: String?,
    41→
    42→    @field:Size(max = 255)
    43→    val adDomain: String?,
    44→
    45→    @field:Size(max = 255)
    46→    val osVersion: String?,
    47→
    48→    @field:Size(max = 255)
    49→    val ip: String?,
    50→
    51→    @field:NotNull
    52→    @field:Valid
    53→    @field:Size(max = 5000)
    54→    val vulnerabilities: List<VulnerabilityDto>
    55→)
    56→

1→package com.secman.dto
     2→
     3→import io.micronaut.serde.annotation.Serdeable
     4→import jakarta.validation.constraints.NotBlank
     5→import jakarta.validation.constraints.NotNull
     6→import java.time.LocalDateTime
     7→
     8→/**
     9→ * DTO representing a vulnerability from CrowdStrike Falcon API
    10→ *
    11→ * Related to: Feature 015-we-have-currently (CrowdStrike System Vulnerability Lookup)
    12→ */
    13→@Serdeable
    14→data class CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityDto(
    15→    /**
    16→     * Unique vulnerability ID from CrowdStrike
    17→     */
    18→    @field:NotBlank
    19→    val id: String,
    20→
    21→    /**
    22→     * System hostname
    23→     */
    24→    @field:NotBlank
    25→    val hostname: String,
    26→
    27→    /**
    28→     * System IP address (nullable)
    29→     */
    30→    val ip: String?,
    31→
    32→    /**
    33→     * CVE identifier (nullable)
    34→     */
    35→    val cveId: String?,
    36→
    37→    /**
    38→     * CVSS severity level (Critical, High, Medium, Low, Informational)
    39→     */
    40→    @field:NotBlank
    41→    val severity: String,
    42→
    43→    /**
    44→     * Numeric CVSS score (0.0-10.0, nullable)
    45→     */
    46→    val cvssScore: Double?,
    47→
    48→    /**
    49→     * Vulnerable product and version (nullable)
    50→     */
    51→    val affectedProduct: String?,
    52→
    53→    /**
    54→     * Days since detection (calculated, e.g., "15 days")
    55→     */
    56→    val daysOpen: String?,
    57→
    58→    /**
    59→     * Detection timestamp (ISO 8601)
    60→     */
    61→    @field:NotNull
    62→    val detectedAt: LocalDateTime,
    63→
    64→    /**
    65→     * Patch publication date from vendor (nullable, from CrowdStrike API)
    66→     */
    67→    val patchPublicationDate: LocalDateTime? = null,
    68→
    69→    /**
    70→     * Vulnerability status (always "open" for this query)
    71→     */
    72→    @field:NotBlank
    73→    val status: String,
    74→
    75→    /**
    76→     * Whether this vulnerability matches an active exception
    77→     */
    78→    @field:NotNull
    79→    val hasException: Boolean,
    80→
    81→    /**
    82→     * Reason for exception if hasException is true (nullable)
    83→     */
    84→    val exceptionReason: String? = null
    85→)
    86→

1→package com.secman.dto
     2→
     3→import io.micronaut.serde.annotation.Serdeable
     4→import jakarta.validation.constraints.NotBlank
     5→import jakarta.validation.constraints.Size
     6→
     7→/**
     8→ * Request DTO for querying CrowdStrike vulnerabilities by hostname
     9→ *
    10→ * Related to: Feature 015-we-have-currently (CrowdStrike System Vulnerability Lookup)
    11→ */
    12→@Serdeable
    13→data class CrowdStrikeQueryRequest(
    14→    /**
    15→     * System hostname to query (case-sensitive as it appears in CrowdStrike)
    16→     */
    17→    @field:NotBlank(message = "Hostname cannot be blank")
    18→    @field:Size(min = 1, max = 255, message = "Hostname must be between 1 and 255 characters")
    19→    val hostname: String
    20→)
    21→

content.content:
1→package com.secman.dto
     2→
     3→import io.micronaut.serde.annotation.Serdeable
     4→import jakarta.validation.constraints.NotBlank
     5→import jakarta.validation.constraints.NotNull
     6→import java.time.LocalDateTime
     7→
     8→/**
     9→ * Response DTO for CrowdStrike vulnerability query
    10→ *
    11→ * Related to:
    12→ * - Feature 015-we-have-currently (CrowdStrike System Vulnerability Lookup)
    13→ * - Feature 041-falcon-instance-lookup (AWS Instance ID queries)
    14→ */
    15→@Serdeable
    16→data class CrowdStrikeQueryResponse(
    17→    /**
    18→     * Echoed hostname from request
    19→     */
    20→    @field:NotBlank
    21→    val hostname: String,
    22→
    23→    /**
    24→     * AWS EC2 Instance ID (Feature 041)
    25→     *
    26→     * Populated when querying by instance ID
    27→     * Null for hostname queries
    28→     */
    29→    val instanceId: String? = null,
    30→
    31→    /**
    32→     * Number of CrowdStrike devices found with this instance ID (Feature 041)
    33→     *
    34→     * Typically 1, rarely 2+ (during instance lifecycle transitions)
    35→     * Null for hostname queries
    36→     */
    37→    val deviceCount: Int? = null,
    38→
    39→    /**
    40→     * AWS account ID associated with the asset (from cloudAccountId field).
    41→     * Null if not available (e.g., non-AWS assets or CrowdStrike API queries).
    42→     */
    43→    val cloudAccountId: String? = null,
    44→
    45→    /**
    46→     * List of vulnerabilities found (empty if none)
    47→     */
    48→    @field:NotNull
    49→    val vulnerabilities: List<CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityDto>,
    50→
    51→    /**
    52→     * Total count from CrowdStrike (may exceed list size if limited to 1000)
    53→     */
    54→    @field:NotNull
    55→    val totalCount: Int,
    56→
    57→    /**
    58→     * Timestamp when query was executed (ISO 8601)
    59→     */
    60→    @field:NotNull
    61→    val queriedAt: LocalDateTime
    62→)
    63→

1→package com.secman.dto
     2→
     3→import com.secman.domain.CrowdStrikeImportHistory
     4→import io.micronaut.serde.annotation.Serdeable
     5→import java.time.LocalDateTime
     6→
     7→/**
     8→ * DTO exposing the latest CrowdStrike import execution metadata.
     9→ *
    10→ * Enables clients to understand how fresh the CrowdStrike-backed datasets are.
    11→ */
    12→@Serdeable
    13→data class CrowdStrikeImportStatusDto(
    14→    val importedAt: LocalDateTime,
    15→    val importedBy: String?,
    16→    val serversProcessed: Int,
    17→    val serversCreated: Int,
    18→    val serversUpdated: Int,
    19→    val vulnerabilitiesImported: Int,
    20→    val vulnerabilitiesSkipped: Int,
    21→    val vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDate: Int,
    22→    val errorCount: Int
    23→) {
    24→    companion object {
    25→        fun fromEntity(entity: CrowdStrikeImportHistory): CrowdStrikeImportStatusDto {
    26→            return CrowdStrikeImportStatusDto(
    27→                importedAt = entity.importedAt ?: LocalDateTime.now(),
    28→                importedBy = entity.importedBy,
    29→                serversProcessed = entity.serversProcessed,
    30→                serversCreated = entity.serversCreated,
    31→                serversUpdated = entity.serversUpdated,
    32→                vulnerabilitiesImported = entity.vulnerabilitiesImported,
    33→                vulnerabilitiesSkipped = entity.vulnerabilitiesSkipped,
    34→                vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDate = entity.vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDate,
    35→                errorCount = entity.errorCount
    36→            )
    37→        }
    38→    }
    39→}
    40→

1→package com.secman.dto
     2→
     3→import io.micronaut.serde.annotation.Serdeable
     4→import jakarta.validation.Valid
     5→import jakarta.validation.constraints.NotBlank
     6→import jakarta.validation.constraints.NotNull
     7→import jakarta.validation.constraints.Size
     8→
     9→/**
    10→ * Request DTO for /api/crowdstrike/vulnerabilities/save endpoint
    11→ *
    12→ * Feature: 032-servers-query-import
    13→ * Spec reference: FR-015a
    14→ *
    15→ * Contains server metadata and its associated vulnerabilities for batch import.
    16→ * Supports transaction-per-server logic with atomic delete+insert operations.
    17→ *
    18→ * @property hostname Server hostname (used as Asset.name, unique identifier)
    19→ * @property groups Comma-separated group names from CrowdStrike
    20→ * @property cloudAccountId AWS/cloud account ID
    21→ * @property cloudInstanceId Cloud instance identifier
    22→ * @property adDomain Active Directory domain
    23→ * @property osVersion Operating system version
    24→ * @property ip IP address
    25→ * @property vulnerabilities List of vulnerabilities for this server
    26→ */
    27→@Serdeable
    28→data class CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityBatchDto(
    29→    @field:NotBlank
    30→    @field:Size(min = 1, max = 255)
    31→    val hostname: String,
    32→
    33→    @field:Size(max = 512)
    34→    val groups: String?,
    35→
    36→    @field:Size(max = 255)
    37→    val cloudAccountId: String?,
    38→
    39→    @field:Size(max = 255)
    40→    val cloudInstanceId: String?,
    41→
    42→    @field:Size(max = 255)
    43→    val adDomain: String?,
    44→
    45→    @field:Size(max = 255)
    46→    val osVersion: String?,
    47→
    48→    @field:Size(max = 255)
    49→    val ip: String?,
    50→
    51→    @field:NotNull
    52→    @field:Valid
    53→    @field:Size(max = 5000)
    54→    val vulnerabilities: List<VulnerabilityDto>
    55→)
    56→

1→package com.secman.dto
     2→
     3→import io.micronaut.serde.annotation.Serdeable
     4→import jakarta.validation.constraints.NotBlank
     5→import jakarta.validation.constraints.NotNull
     6→import java.time.LocalDateTime
     7→
     8→/**
     9→ * DTO representing a vulnerability from CrowdStrike Falcon API
    10→ *
    11→ * Related to: Feature 015-we-have-currently (CrowdStrike System Vulnerability Lookup)
    12→ */
    13→@Serdeable
    14→data class CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityDto(
    15→    /**
    16→     * Unique vulnerability ID from CrowdStrike
    17→     */
    18→    @field:NotBlank
    19→    val id: String,
    20→
    21→    /**
    22→     * System hostname
    23→     */
    24→    @field:NotBlank
    25→    val hostname: String,
    26→
    27→    /**
    28→     * System IP address (nullable)
    29→     */
    30→    val ip: String?,
    31→
    32→    /**
    33→     * CVE identifier (nullable)
    34→     */
    35→    val cveId: String?,
    36→
    37→    /**
    38→     * CVSS severity level (Critical, High, Medium, Low, Informational)
    39→     */
    40→    @field:NotBlank
    41→    val severity: String,
    42→
    43→    /**
    44→     * Numeric CVSS score (0.0-10.0, nullable)
    45→     */
    46→    val cvssScore: Double?,
    47→
    48→    /**
    49→     * Vulnerable product and version (nullable)
    50→     */
    51→    val affectedProduct: String?,
    52→
    53→    /**
    54→     * Days since detection (calculated, e.g., "15 days")
    55→     */
    56→    val daysOpen: String?,
    57→
    58→    /**
    59→     * Detection timestamp (ISO 8601)
    60→     */
    61→    @field:NotNull
    62→    val detectedAt: LocalDateTime,
    63→
    64→    /**
    65→     * Patch publication date from vendor (nullable, from CrowdStrike API)
    66→     */
    67→    val patchPublicationDate: LocalDateTime? = null,
    68→
    69→    /**
    70→     * Vulnerability status (always "open" for this query)
    71→     */
    72→    @field:NotBlank
    73→    val status: String,
    74→
    75→    /**
    76→     * Whether this vulnerability matches an active exception
    77→     */
    78→    @field:NotNull
    79→    val hasException: Boolean,
    80→
    81→    /**
    82→     * Reason for exception if hasException is true (nullable)
    83→     */
    84→    val exceptionReason: String? = null
    85→)
    86→

1→package com.secman.dto
     2→
     3→import io.micronaut.serde.annotation.Serdeable
     4→import jakarta.validation.Valid
     5→import jakarta.validation.constraints.NotBlank
     6→import jakarta.validation.constraints.NotEmpty
     7→import jakarta.validation.constraints.Size
     8→
     9→/**
    10→ * Request DTO for saving CrowdStrike vulnerabilities to database
    11→ *
    12→ * Related to:
    13→ * - Feature 015-we-have-currently (CrowdStrike System Vulnerability Lookup)
    14→ * - Feature 041-falcon-instance-lookup (AWS Instance ID Support)
    15→ */
    16→@Serdeable
    17→data class CrowdStrikeSaveRequest(
    18→    /**
    19→     * System hostname (used for asset matching/creation)
    20→     */
    21→    @field:NotBlank(message = "Hostname is required")
    22→    @field:Size(min = 1, max = 255, message = "Hostname must be between 1 and 255 characters")
    23→    val hostname: String,
    24→
    25→    /**
    26→     * List of vulnerabilities to save (at least one required)
    27→     */
    28→    @field:NotEmpty(message = "At least one vulnerability is required")
    29→    @field:Valid
    30→    val vulnerabilities: List<CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityDto>,
    31→
    32→    /**
    33→     * AWS EC2 Instance ID (optional, for instance ID queries)
    34→     * Feature: 041-falcon-instance-lookup
    35→     * Task: T047
    36→     *
    37→     * When present, this value will be stored in the asset's cloudInstanceId field
    38→     * for asset enrichment and tracking purposes.
    39→     */
    40→    @field:Size(max = 19, message = "Instance ID must not exceed 19 characters")
    41→    val instanceId: String? = null
    42→)
    43→

content.content:
1→package com.secman.cli.service
     2→
     3→import com.secman.crowdstrike.client.CrowdStrikeApiClient
     4→import com.secman.crowdstrike.dto.CrowdStrikeQueryResponse
     5→import com.secman.crowdstrike.dto.FalconConfigDto
     6→import jakarta.inject.Singleton
     7→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
     8→
     9→/**
    10→ * Service for polling CrowdStrike API for HIGH and CRITICAL vulnerabilities
    11→ *
    12→ * Functionality:
    13→ * - Query all devices or specific hostnames
    14→ * - Filter for HIGH and CRITICAL severity
    15→ * - Store results via VulnerabilityStorageService
    16→ * - Handle pagination and rate limiting
    17→ *
    18→ * Related to: Feature 026-crowdstrike-polling-monitor
    19→ * Tasks: T5-T9
    20→ */
    21→@Singleton
    22→class CrowdStrikePollerService(
    23→    private val apiClient: CrowdStrikeApiClient,
    24→    private val storageService: VulnerabilityStorageService
    25→) {
    26→    private val log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(CrowdStrikePollerService::class.java)
    27→    
    28→    var backendUrl: String? = null
    29→    var dryRun: Boolean = false
    30→    var verbose: Boolean = false
    31→    
    32→    /**
    33→     * Poll all devices in CrowdStrike for HIGH/CRITICAL vulnerabilities
    34→     *
    35→     * Task: T5
    36→     *
    37→     * @param config CrowdStrike configuration
    38→     * @return PollResult with statistics
    39→     */
    40→    fun pollAllDevices(config: FalconConfigDto): PollResult {
    41→        log.info("Polling all devices for HIGH and CRITICAL vulnerabilities")
    42→        
    43→        // For now, we need to query specific hostnames since CrowdStrike API
    44→        // requires device ID or hostname. In production, you would:
    45→        // 1. First query /devices/queries/devices/v1 to get all device IDs
    46→        // 2. Then query each device for vulnerabilities
    47→        // This is a simplified implementation that requires hostnames
    48→        
    49→        log.warn("Poll all devices not fully implemented - requires hostname list")
    50→        return PollResult(
    51→            devicesQueried = 0,
    52→            totalVulnerabilities = 0,
    53→            stored = 0,
    54→            skipped = 0,
    55→            errors = emptyList()
    56→        )
    57→    }
    58→    
    59→    /**
    60→     * Poll specific hostnames for HIGH/CRITICAL vulnerabilities
    61→     *
    62→     * Task: T5-T6
    63→     *
    64→     * @param hostnames List of hostnames to query
    65→     * @param config CrowdStrike configuration
    66→     * @return PollResult with statistics
    67→     */
    68→    fun pollHostnames(hostnames: List<String>, config: FalconConfigDto): PollResult {
    69→        log.info("Polling {} hostnames for HIGH and CRITICAL vulnerabilities", hostnames.size)
    70→        
    71→        var devicesQueried = 0
    72→        var totalVulnerabilities = 0
    73→        var stored = 0
    74→        var skipped = 0
    75→        val errors = mutableListOf<String>()
    76→        
    77→        for (hostname in hostnames) {
    78→            try {
    79→                log.debug("Querying hostname: {}", hostname)
    80→                
    81→                // Query CrowdStrike API
    82→                val response = apiClient.queryAllVulnerabilities(hostname, config, limit = 5000)
    83→                devicesQueried++
    84→                
    85→                // Filter for HIGH and CRITICAL severity (Task: T6)
    86→                val highCriticalVulns = filterHighCritical(response)
    87→                totalVulnerabilities += highCriticalVulns.size
    88→                
    89→                if (highCriticalVulns.isEmpty()) {
    90→                    log.debug("No HIGH/CRITICAL vulnerabilities found for {}", hostname)
    91→                    continue
    92→                }
    93→                
    94→                log.info("Found {} HIGH/CRITICAL vulnerabilities for {}", highCriticalVulns.size, hostname)
    95→                
    96→                // Store vulnerabilities if not in dry-run mode
    97→                if (!dryRun) {
    98→                    val storeResult = storageService.storeVulnerabilities(
    99→                        hostname = hostname,
   100→                        vulnerabilities = highCriticalVulns,
   101→                        backendUrl = backendUrl
   102→                    )
   103→                    stored += storeResult.stored
   104→                    skipped += storeResult.skipped
   105→                } else {
   106→                    log.debug("Dry run mode - skipping storage")
   107→                }
   108→            } catch (e: Exception) {
   109→                val errorMsg = "Failed to poll $hostname: ${e.message}"
   110→                log.error(errorMsg, e)
   111→                errors.add(errorMsg)
   112→            }
   113→        }
   114→        
   115→        log.info("Poll complete: devices={}, vulnerabilities={}, stored={}, skipped={}, errors={}",
   116→            devicesQueried, totalVulnerabilities, stored, skipped, errors.size)
   117→        
   118→        return PollResult(
   119→            devicesQueried = devicesQueried,
   120→            totalVulnerabilities = totalVulnerabilities,
   121→            stored = stored,
   122→            skipped = skipped,
   123→            errors = errors
   124→        )
   125→    }
   126→    
   127→    /**
   128→     * Filter vulnerabilities for HIGH and CRITICAL severity
   129→     *
   130→     * Task: T6
   131→     *
   132→     * @param response CrowdStrike query response
   133→     * @return Filtered response with only HIGH/CRITICAL vulnerabilities
   134→     */
   135→    private fun filterHighCritical(response: CrowdStrikeQueryResponse): List<com.secman.crowdstrike.dto.CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityDto> {
   136→        return response.vulnerabilities.filter { vuln ->
   137→            vuln.severity.uppercase() in listOf("HIGH", "CRITICAL")
   138→        }
   139→    }
   140→}
   141→
   142→/**
   143→ * Result of a poll operation
   144→ */
   145→data class PollResult(
   146→    val devicesQueried: Int,
   147→    val totalVulnerabilities: Int,
   148→    val stored: Int,
   149→    val skipped: Int,
   150→    val errors: List<String>
   151→)
   152→

     7→
     8→/**
     9→ * Interface for CrowdStrike Falcon API client
    10→ *
    11→ * Defines contract for querying CrowdStrike Spotlight API for vulnerabilities
    12→ */
    13→interface CrowdStrikeApiClient {
    14→    /**
    15→     * Query vulnerabilities for a specific hostname
    16→     *
    17→     * Task: T030
    18→     *
    19→     * @param hostname System hostname to query
    20→     * @param config CrowdStrike Falcon configuration
    21→     * @return CrowdStrikeQueryResponse with vulnerabilities
    22→     */
    23→    fun queryVulnerabilities(hostname: String, config: FalconConfigDto): CrowdStrikeQueryResponse
    24→
    25→    /**
    26→     * Query all vulnerabilities with automatic pagination
    27→     *
    28→     * Task: T032
    29→     *
    30→     * @param hostname System hostname to query
    31→     * @param config CrowdStrike Falcon configuration
    32→     * @param limit Page size (default: 100)
    33→     * @return CrowdStrikeQueryResponse with all vulnerabilities
    34→     */
    35→    fun queryAllVulnerabilities(
    36→        hostname: String,
    37→        config: FalconConfigDto,
    38→        limit: Int = 100
    39→    ): CrowdStrikeQueryResponse
    40→
    41→    /**
    42→     * Query servers with filters (device type, severity, days open)
    43→     *
    44→     * Feature: 032-servers-query-import
    45→     * Task: T007, T008
    46→     * Spec reference: FR-002, FR-003, FR-004, FR-001a
    47→     *
    48→     * @param hostnames Optional list of specific hostnames to query (null = all servers)
    49→     * @param deviceType Device type filter (e.g., "SERVER")
    50→     * @param severity Severity filter (e.g., "HIGH,CRITICAL")
    51→     * @param minDaysOpen Minimum days open filter (e.g., 30)
    52→     * @param config CrowdStrike Falcon configuration
    53→     * @param limit Page size for pagination
    54→     * @return CrowdStrikeQueryResponse with filtered vulnerabilities
    55→     */
    56→    fun queryServersWithFilters(
    57→        hostnames: List<String>? = null,
    58→        deviceType: String = "SERVER",
    59→        severity: String = "HIGH,CRITICAL",
    60→        minDaysOpen: Int = 30,
    61→        config: FalconConfigDto,
    62→        limit: Int = 100,
    63→        lastSeenDays: Int = 0
    64→    ): CrowdStrikeQueryResponse
    65→
    66→    /**
    67→     * Get the authorization token
    68→     *
    69→     * @param config CrowdStrike Falcon configuration
    70→     * @return AuthToken for API requests
    71→     */
    72→    fun getAuthToken(config: FalconConfigDto): AuthToken
    73→
    74→    /**
    75→     * Query vulnerabilities by AWS EC2 Instance ID
    76→     *
    77→     * Feature: 041-falcon-instance-lookup
    78→     * Task: T011
    79→     *
    80→     * @param instanceId AWS EC2 Instance ID (format: i-XXXXXXXXX...)
    81→     * @param config CrowdStrike Falcon configuration
    82→     * @return CrowdStrikeQueryResponse with vulnerabilities from all devices with this instance ID
    83→     */
    84→    fun queryVulnerabilitiesByInstanceId(instanceId: String, config: FalconConfigDto): CrowdStrikeQueryResponse
    85→
    86→    /**
    87→     * Query vulnerabilities by Active Directory domains
    88→     *
    89→     * Feature: 042-domain-vulnerabilities-view
    90→     *
    91→     * @param domains List of AD domain names (e.g., ["CONTOSO", "EXAMPLE"])
    92→     * @param severity Severity filter (e.g., "HIGH,CRITICAL")
    93→     * @param minDaysOpen Minimum days open filter
    94→     * @param config CrowdStrike Falcon configuration
    95→     * @param limit Page size for pagination
    96→     * @return CrowdStrikeQueryResponse with vulnerabilities from all devices in these domains
    97→     */
    98→    fun queryVulnerabilitiesByDomains(
    99→        domains: List<String>,
   100→        severity: String = "HIGH,CRITICAL",
   101→        minDaysOpen: Int = 0,
   102→        config: FalconConfigDto,
   103→        limit: Int = 1000
   104→    ): CrowdStrikeQueryResponse
   105→
   106→    /**
   107→     * Query servers with filters, processing results in streaming batches to reduce peak memory usage.
   108→     *
   109→     * Instead of accumulating all vulnerabilities in memory, this method processes device ID

   111→     * This reduces peak memory from O(all_vulns) to O(batch_vulns).
   112→     *
   113→     * @param deviceType Device type filter (e.g., "SERVER")
   114→     * @param severity Severity filter (e.g., "HIGH,CRITICAL")

   123→    fun queryServersWithFiltersStreaming(

   129→        lastSeenDays: Int = 0,
   130→        deviceBatchSize: Int = 200,
   131→        batchProcessor: (List<CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityDto>) -> Unit
   132→    ): Int
   133→}

1→package com.secman.crowdstrike.client
     2→
     3→import com.secman.crowdstrike.auth.CrowdStrikeAuthService
     4→import com.secman.crowdstrike.dto.CrowdStrikeQueryResponse
     5→import com.secman.crowdstrike.dto.CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityDto
     6→import com.secman.crowdstrike.dto.DeviceType
     7→import com.secman.crowdstrike.dto.FalconConfigDto
     8→import com.secman.crowdstrike.exception.CrowdStrikeException
     9→import com.secman.crowdstrike.exception.NotFoundException
    10→import com.secman.crowdstrike.exception.RateLimitException
    11→import com.secman.crowdstrike.model.AuthToken
    12→import io.micronaut.context.annotation.Value
    13→import io.micronaut.http.HttpRequest
    14→import io.micronaut.http.HttpResponse
    15→import io.micronaut.http.client.HttpClient
    16→import io.micronaut.http.client.annotation.Client
    17→import io.micronaut.http.uri.UriBuilder
    18→import io.micronaut.retry.annotation.Retryable
    19→import jakarta.inject.Singleton
    20→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
    21→import java.time.Instant
    22→import java.time.LocalDateTime
    23→import java.time.ZoneId
    24→import java.time.format.DateTimeFormatter
    25→import java.util.concurrent.Callable
    26→import java.util.concurrent.ExecutionException
    27→import java.util.concurrent.ExecutorService
    28→import java.util.concurrent.Executors
    29→import java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicInteger
    30→
    31→/**
    32→ * CrowdStrike Falcon API client implementation
    33→ *
    34→ * Provides:
    35→ * - Spotlight API queries for vulnerability data
    36→ * - Automatic retry with exponential backoff for transient errors
    37→ * - Pagination support for large result sets
    38→ * - Response mapping to shared DTOs
    39→ *
    40→ * Related to: Feature 023-create-in-the (CrowdStrike CLI)
    41→ * Tasks: T029-T033
    42→ */
    43→@Singleton
    44→open class CrowdStrikeApiClientImpl(
    45→    @Client("https://api.crowdstrike.com")
    46→    private val httpClient: HttpClient,
    47→    private val authService: CrowdStrikeAuthService
    48→) : CrowdStrikeApiClient {
    49→
    50→    private val log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(CrowdStrikeApiClientImpl::class.java)
    51→
    52→    @field:Value("\${secman.crowdstrike.batch-size:20}")
    53→    protected var configuredBatchSize: Int = 20
    54→
    55→    @field:Value("\${secman.crowdstrike.max-parallel-batches:4}")
    56→    protected var configuredMaxParallelBatches: Int = 4
    57→
    58→    /**
    59→     * Query vulnerabilities for a specific hostname
    60→     *
    61→     * Task: T030
    62→     *
    63→     * @param hostname System hostname to query
    64→     * @param config CrowdStrike configuration
    65→     * @return CrowdStrikeQueryResponse with vulnerabilities
    66→     * @throws NotFoundException if hostname not found in CrowdStrike
    67→     * @throws RateLimitException if rate limit exceeded
    68→     * @throws CrowdStrikeException for other API errors
    69→     */
    70→    override fun queryVulnerabilities(hostname: String, config: FalconConfigDto): CrowdStrikeQueryResponse {
    71→        require(hostname.isNotBlank()) { "Hostname cannot be blank" }
    72→
    73→        log.info("Querying CrowdStrike for vulnerabilities: hostname={}", hostname)
    74→
    75→        return try {
    76→            // Authenticate
    77→            val token = getAuthToken(config)
    78→
    79→            // Get device ID from hostname
    80→            val deviceId = getDeviceIdByHostname(hostname, token)
    81→                ?: throw NotFoundException("Hostname not found in CrowdStrike: $hostname")
    82→
    83→            log.info("Using device ID '{}' for hostname '{}'", deviceId, hostname)
    84→
    85→            // Query Spotlight API - pass hostname so it can be included in results
    86→            val vulnerabilityDtos = querySpotlightApi(deviceId, hostname, token)
    87→
    88→            log.info("Successfully queried CrowdStrike: hostname={}, count={}", hostname, vulnerabilityDtos.size)
    89→
    90→            CrowdStrikeQueryResponse(
    91→                hostname = hostname,
    92→                vulnerabilities = vulnerabilityDtos,
    93→                totalCount = vulnerabilityDtos.size,
    94→                queriedAt = LocalDateTime.now()
    95→            )
    96→        } catch (e: CrowdStrikeException) {
    97→            log.error("CrowdStrike query failed: hostname={}, error={}", hostname, e.message)
    98→            throw e
    99→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   100→            log.error("Unexpected error querying CrowdStrike: hostname={}", hostname, e)
   101→            throw CrowdStrikeException("Failed to query vulnerabilities for $hostname: ${e.message}", e)
   102→        }
   103→    }
   104→
   105→    /**
   106→     * Query all vulnerabilities with automatic pagination
   107→     *
   108→     * Task: T032
   109→     *
   110→     * @param hostname System hostname to query
   111→     * @param config CrowdStrike configuration
   112→     * @param limit Page size (default: 100)
   113→     * @return CrowdStrikeQueryResponse with all vulnerabilities
   114→     */
   115→    override fun queryAllVulnerabilities(
   116→        hostname: String,
   117→        config: FalconConfigDto,
   118→        limit: Int
   119→    ): CrowdStrikeQueryResponse {
   120→        require(limit in 1..5000) { "Limit must be between 1 and 5000" }
   121→
   122→        log.info("Querying CrowdStrike for all vulnerabilities (paginated): hostname={}, limit={}", hostname, limit)
   123→
   124→        var allVulnerabilities = mutableListOf<CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityDto>()
   125→        var offset = 0
   126→        var hasMore = true
   127→
   128→        while (hasMore) {
   129→            try {
   130→                val response = queryVulnerabilities(hostname, config)
   131→                allVulnerabilities.addAll(response.vulnerabilities)
   132→
   133→                // For now, return single page (pagination requires CrowdStrike cursor/offset support)
   134→                hasMore = false
   135→            } catch (e: CrowdStrikeException) {
   136→                // Don't log stack trace for known CrowdStrike exceptions - just rethrow
   137→                throw e
   138→            } catch (e: Exception) {
   139→                log.error("Error during pagination: offset={}", offset, e)
   140→                throw e
   141→            }
   142→        }
   143→
   144→        return CrowdStrikeQueryResponse(
   145→            hostname = hostname,
   146→            vulnerabilities = allVulnerabilities,
   147→            totalCount = allVulnerabilities.size,
   148→            queriedAt = LocalDateTime.now()
   149→        )
   150→    }
