1→package com.secman.service
     2→
     3→import com.secman.config.VulnerabilitySettings
     4→import com.secman.domain.Asset
     5→import com.secman.domain.CrowdStrikeImportHistory
     6→import com.secman.domain.Vulnerability
     7→import com.secman.dto.CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityBatchDto
     8→import com.secman.dto.ImportStatisticsDto
     9→import com.secman.dto.CrowdStrikeImportStatusDto
    10→import com.secman.dto.VulnerabilityDto
    11→import com.secman.repository.AssetRepository
    12→import com.secman.repository.VulnerabilityRepository
    13→import com.secman.repository.CrowdStrikeImportHistoryRepository
    14→import jakarta.inject.Singleton
    15→import jakarta.persistence.EntityManager
    16→import jakarta.persistence.LockModeType
    17→import jakarta.transaction.Transactional
    18→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
    19→import java.time.LocalDateTime
    20→import java.time.temporal.ChronoUnit
    21→
    22→/**
    23→ * Service for importing CrowdStrike server vulnerabilities with automatic asset creation
    24→ *
    25→ * Provides functionality to:
    26→ * - Find or create Asset records from CrowdStrike server data
    27→ * - Import vulnerabilities with transactional replace pattern (delete old + insert new)
    28→ * - Track import statistics (servers created/updated, vulnerabilities imported/skipped)
    29→ * - Handle per-server transactions with rollback on failure
    30→ * - Preserve existing asset metadata (owner, description, workgroups) when reusing assets
    31→ *
    32→ * Feature: 032-servers-query-import
    33→ * Tasks: T019, T020, T021, T022
    34→ * Spec reference: FR-008, FR-010, FR-011, FR-012, FR-013, FR-015
    35→ */
    36→@Singleton
    37→open class CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityImportService(
    38→    private val assetRepository: AssetRepository,
    39→    private val vulnerabilityRepository: VulnerabilityRepository,
    40→    private val importHistoryRepository: CrowdStrikeImportHistoryRepository,
    41→    private val materializedViewRefreshService: MaterializedViewRefreshService,
    42→    private val vulnerabilitySettings: VulnerabilitySettings,
    43→    private val entityManager: EntityManager
    44→) {
    45→    private val log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityImportService::class.java)
    46→
    47→    /**
    48→     * Provider for self-reference to ensure @Transactional works on internal method calls.
    49→     * Using Provider<T> avoids circular dependency during bean construction.
    50→     * Feature 053: Fix AOP proxy bypass for transactional delete-insert pattern
    51→     */
    52→    @jakarta.inject.Inject
    53→    private lateinit var selfProvider: jakarta.inject.Provider<CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityImportService>
    54→
    55→    /**
    56→     * Import batch of server vulnerabilities from CrowdStrike
    57→     *
    58→     * Task: T022
    59→     * Spec reference: FR-008
    60→     *
    61→     * @param batches List of server vulnerability batches
    62→     * @param triggeredBy User/system that triggered the import (for audit trail)
    63→     * @param triggerRefresh Whether to trigger materialized view refresh after import.
    64→     *                       Set to false when importing multiple batches to avoid redundant refreshes.
    65→     *                       The caller should trigger a single refresh after all batches complete.
    66→     * @return ImportStatisticsDto with detailed statistics
    67→     */
    68→    fun importServerVulnerabilities(
    69→        batches: List<CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityBatchDto>,
    70→        triggeredBy: String? = null,
    71→        triggerRefresh: Boolean = true
    72→    ): ImportStatisticsDto {
    73→        log.info("Starting batch import of {} servers", batches.size)
    74→
    75→        var serversCreated = 0
    76→        var serversUpdated = 0
    77→        var vulnerabilitiesImported = 0
    78→        var vulnerabilitiesSkipped = 0
    79→        var vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDate = 0
    80→        val uniqueDomains = mutableSetOf<String>()  // Feature 043: Track unique domains
    81→        val errors = mutableListOf<String>()
    82→
    83→        for (batch in batches) {
    84→            try {
    85→                // Process each server in its own transaction (FR-010)
    86→                // Use selfProvider.get() to ensure @Transactional is applied via AOP proxy (Feature 053)
    87→                val result = selfProvider.get().importVulnerabilitiesForServer(batch)
    88→
    89→                if (result.assetCreated) serversCreated++
    90→                else serversUpdated++
    91→
    92→                vulnerabilitiesImported += result.vulnerabilitiesImported
    93→                vulnerabilitiesSkipped += result.vulnerabilitiesSkipped
    94→                vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDate += result.vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDate
    95→
    96→                // Track unique domains (Feature 043)
    97→                batch.adDomain?.let { domain ->
    98→                    if (domain.isNotBlank()) {
    99→                        uniqueDomains.add(domain.trim().uppercase())
   100→                        log.trace("Tracked domain: {}", domain.trim().uppercase())
   101→                    }
   102→                }
   103→
   104→                log.debug("Imported server '{}': created={}, imported={}, skipped={}, withPatchDate={}, domain={}",
   105→                    batch.hostname, result.assetCreated, result.vulnerabilitiesImported, result.vulnerabilitiesSkipped, result.vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDate, batch.adDomain)
   106→            } catch (e: Exception) {
   107→                val errorMsg = "Failed to import server '${batch.hostname}': ${e.message}"
   108→                log.error(errorMsg, e)
   109→                errors.add(errorMsg)
   110→            }
   111→        }
   112→
   113→        val statistics = ImportStatisticsDto(
   114→            serversProcessed = batches.size,
   115→            serversCreated = serversCreated,
   116→            serversUpdated = serversUpdated,
   117→            vulnerabilitiesImported = vulnerabilitiesImported,
   118→            vulnerabilitiesSkipped = vulnerabilitiesSkipped,
   119→            vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDate = vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDate,
   120→            uniqueDomainCount = uniqueDomains.size,  // Feature 043
   121→            discoveredDomains = uniqueDomains.sorted(),  // Feature 043
   122→            errors = errors
   123→        )
   124→
   125→        log.info("Batch import completed: processed={}, created={}, updated={}, imported={}, skipped={}, withPatchDate={}, uniqueDomains={}, errors={}",
   126→            batches.size, serversCreated, serversUpdated, vulnerabilitiesImported, vulnerabilitiesSkipped, vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDate, uniqueDomains.size, errors.size)
   127→
   128→        recordImportHistory(statistics, triggeredBy)
   129→
   130→        // Trigger async materialized view refresh after import (if requested)
   131→        // Feature: 034-outdated-assets, Task: T101
   132→        // Spec reference: FR-005 (async refresh)
   133→        // Note: When importing multiple batches, set triggerRefresh=false to avoid redundant refreshes.
   134→        // The caller should trigger a single refresh after all batches complete.
   135→        if (triggerRefresh && vulnerabilitiesImported > 0) {
   136→            log.info("Triggering materialized view refresh after importing {} vulnerabilities", vulnerabilitiesImported)
   137→            materializedViewRefreshService.triggerAsyncRefresh("CLI Import - $vulnerabilitiesImported vulnerabilities imported")
   138→        } else if (!triggerRefresh && vulnerabilitiesImported > 0) {
   139→            log.debug("Skipping materialized view refresh (triggerRefresh=false, caller will handle)")
   140→        }
   141→
   142→        return statistics
   143→    }
   144→
   145→    /**
   146→     * Persist import execution metadata for UI consumption.
   147→     */
   148→    private fun recordImportHistory(statistics: ImportStatisticsDto, triggeredBy: String?) {
   149→        try {
   150→            val history = CrowdStrikeImportHistory(
   151→                importedAt = LocalDateTime.now(),
   152→                importedBy = triggeredBy,
   153→                serversProcessed = statistics.serversProcessed,
   154→                serversCreated = statistics.serversCreated,
   155→                serversUpdated = statistics.serversUpdated,
   156→                vulnerabilitiesImported = statistics.vulnerabilitiesImported,
   157→                vulnerabilitiesSkipped = statistics.vulnerabilitiesSkipped,
   158→                vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDate = statistics.vulnerabilitiesWithPatchDate,
   159→                errorCount = statistics.errors.size
   160→            )
   161→            importHistoryRepository.save(history)
   162→            log.info("Recorded CrowdStrike import history entry: {}", history)
   163→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   164→            log.error("Failed to record CrowdStrike import history", e)
   165→        }
   166→    }
   167→
   168→    fun getLatestImportStatus(): CrowdStrikeImportStatusDto? {
   169→        return importHistoryRepository.findLatest()
   170→            ?.let { CrowdStrikeImportStatusDto.fromEntity(it) }
   171→    }
   172→
   173→    /**
   174→     * Import vulnerabilities for a single server with transactional replace pattern
   175→     *
   176→     * **Duplicate Prevention Strategy (Feature 048):**
   177→     * This method prevents duplicate vulnerability entries by implementing a transactional replace pattern:
   178→     * 1. DELETE all existing vulnerabilities for the asset
   179→     * 2. INSERT new vulnerabilities from the import batch
   180→     * 3. All operations wrapped in @Transactional boundary
   181→     *
   182→     * This approach ensures:
   183→     * - **Idempotency**: Running the same import multiple times produces identical results
   184→     * - **No duplicates**: Each (asset, CVE) combination exists exactly once
   185→     * - **Atomicity**: Either all vulnerabilities are replaced, or none are (on failure)
   186→     * - **Remediation tracking**: Removed CVEs indicate vulnerabilities have been patched
   187→     *
   188→     * The transactional replace pattern is preferred over upsert because:
   189→     * - Simpler logic: No need to detect which records to update vs insert
   190→     * - Accurate remediation: Missing CVEs in new import = vulnerability fixed
   191→     * - Better performance: Bulk delete + bulk insert faster than individual upserts
   192→     * - Cleaner state: No orphaned records from complex merge logic
   193→     *
   194→     * **Important Implementation Detail:**
   195→     * The Asset.vulnerabilities relationship MUST NOT use `cascade = [CascadeType.ALL]` or `orphanRemoval = true`.
   196→     * Manual delete-insert operations conflict with JPA cascade behavior, causing newly inserted
   197→     * vulnerabilities to be cascade-deleted when the transaction commits. This service performs
   198→     * explicit vulnerability deletion via `vulnerabilityRepository.deleteByAssetId()` before insertion.
   199→     *
   200→     * Tasks: T021, T024, T025, T026

800→            .coerceAtMost(batches.size)
   801→
   802→        log.info(">>> Stage 2: Querying vulnerabilities (severity={}, minDaysOpen={}, batchSize={}, parallelism={})",
   803→            severity, minDaysOpen, batchSize, parallelism)
   804→        log.info(">>> Stage 2: Split {} device IDs into {} batches", deviceIds.size, batches.size)
   805→
   806→        val allVulnerabilities = mutableListOf<CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityDto>()
   807→
   808→        if (batches.size == 1) {
   809→            allVulnerabilities.addAll(
   810→                queryBatchVulnerabilities(
   811→                    batchIndex = 0,
   812→                    totalBatches = 1,
   813→                    deviceIds = batches.first(),
   814→                    severity = severity,
   815→                    minDaysOpen = minDaysOpen,
   816→                    limit = limit,
   817→                    config = config
   818→                )
   819→            )
   820→        } else {
   821→            val executor = createBatchExecutor(parallelism)
   822→            val futures = batches.mapIndexed { index, batch ->
   823→                executor.submit(Callable {
   824→                    queryBatchVulnerabilities(
   825→                        batchIndex = index,
   826→                        totalBatches = batches.size,
   827→                        deviceIds = batch,
   828→                        severity = severity,
   829→                        minDaysOpen = minDaysOpen,
   830→                        limit = limit,
   831→                        config = config
   832→                    )
   833→                })
   834→            }
   835→
   836→            // Collect results with fault tolerance - continue even if some batches fail
   837→            val failedBatches = mutableListOf<Int>()
   838→            val errors = mutableListOf<String>()
   839→
   840→            try {
   841→                futures.forEachIndexed { index, future ->
   842→                    try {
   843→                        allVulnerabilities.addAll(future.get())
   844→                    } catch (e: ExecutionException) {
   845→                        val cause = e.cause
   846→                        val errorMsg = "Batch ${index + 1}/${batches.size} failed: ${cause?.message ?: e.message}"
   847→                        log.warn(">>> $errorMsg")
   848→                        failedBatches.add(index + 1)
   849→                        errors.add(errorMsg)
   850→                        // Continue with other batches instead of failing entirely
   851→                    } catch (e: java.util.concurrent.CancellationException) {
   852→                        log.warn(">>> Batch ${index + 1}/${batches.size} was cancelled")
   853→                        failedBatches.add(index + 1)
   854→                    }
   855→                }
   856→            } catch (e: InterruptedException) {
   857→                log.warn(">>> Batch processing interrupted, cancelling remaining futures")
   858→                futures.forEach { it.cancel(true) }
   859→                Thread.currentThread().interrupt()
   860→                // Don't throw - return partial results
   861→            } finally {
   862→                executor.shutdown()
   863→            }
   864→
   865→            // Log summary of failures
   866→            if (failedBatches.isNotEmpty()) {
   867→                log.warn(">>> {} of {} batches failed: {}", failedBatches.size, batches.size, failedBatches.joinToString(", "))
   868→                log.warn(">>> Continuing with {} vulnerabilities from successful batches", allVulnerabilities.size)
   869→            }
   870→        }
   871→
   872→        log.info(">>> Stage 2 complete: {} total vulnerabilities found across {} batches",
   873→            allVulnerabilities.size, batches.size)
   874→
   875→        return allVulnerabilities
   876→    }
   877→
   878→    private fun queryBatchVulnerabilities(
   879→        batchIndex: Int,
   880→        totalBatches: Int,
   881→        deviceIds: List<String>,
   882→        severity: String,
   883→        minDaysOpen: Int,
   884→        limit: Int,
   885→        config: FalconConfigDto
   886→    ): List<CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityDto> {
   887→        var token = getAuthToken(config)
   888→        val metadataByDeviceId = resolveDeviceMetadata(deviceIds, token)
   889→        val severityFilter = buildSeverityFilter(severity)
   890→        val deviceIdFilter = buildDeviceIdFilter(deviceIds)
   891→        val fqlFilter = "$deviceIdFilter+status:'open'+$severityFilter"
   892→        val batchVulnerabilities = mutableListOf<CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityDto>()
   893→        val effectiveLimit = limit.coerceAtMost(5000)
   894→        val maxPagesPerBatch = 50
   895→        var afterToken: String? = null
   896→        var hasMore = true
   897→        var pageCount = 0
   898→
   899→        log.debug(">>> Batch {}/{} starting with {} device IDs", batchIndex + 1, totalBatches, deviceIds.size)
   900→
   901→        // Retry tracking for transient network errors (Feature 053)
   902→        val maxRetries = 3
   903→        val retryCount = mutableMapOf<Int, Int>()  // page -> retry count
   904→
   905→        // Helper to handle retry logic for transient errors
   906→        fun retryTransientError(batchIdx: Int, totalBatch: Int, page: Int, errorType: String, e: Exception): Boolean {
   907→            val currentRetries = retryCount.getOrDefault(page, 0)
   908→            if (currentRetries < maxRetries) {
   909→                retryCount[page] = currentRetries + 1
   910→                val backoffMs = (currentRetries + 1) * 2000L  // 2s, 4s, 6s
   911→                log.warn(">>> Batch {}/{} page {}: {} - {}. Retry {}/{} after {}ms",
   912→                    batchIdx + 1, totalBatch, page, errorType, e.message, currentRetries + 1, maxRetries, backoffMs)
   913→                try {
   914→                    Thread.sleep(backoffMs)
   915→                } catch (ie: InterruptedException) {
   916→                    Thread.currentThread().interrupt()
   917→                    return false
   918→                }
   919→                return true
   920→            }
   921→            log.error(">>> Batch {}/{} page {}: {} - {}. Max retries ({}) exceeded",
   922→                batchIdx + 1, totalBatch, page, errorType, e.message, maxRetries)
   923→            return false
   924→        }
   925→
   926→        while (hasMore && pageCount < maxPagesPerBatch) {
   927→            if (Thread.currentThread().isInterrupted) {
   928→                throw InterruptedException("Batch ${batchIndex + 1} interrupted")
   929→            }
   930→
   931→            if (token.isExpiringSoon(bufferSeconds = 180)) {
   932→                log.debug(">>> Batch {}/{}: Token expiring soon, refreshing", batchIndex + 1, totalBatches)
   933→                token = getAuthToken(config)
   934→            }
   935→
   936→            val uri = UriBuilder.of("/spotlight/combined/vulnerabilities/v1")
   937→                .queryParam("filter", fqlFilter)
   938→                .queryParam("limit", effectiveLimit)
   939→                .queryParam("facet", "cve")
   940→                .apply {
   941→                    if (afterToken != null) {
   942→                        queryParam("after", afterToken)
   943→                    }
   944→                }
   945→                .build()
   946→
   947→            val request = HttpRequest.GET<Any>(uri.toString())
   948→                .header("Authorization", "Bearer ${token.accessToken}")
   949→                .header("Accept", "application/json")
   950→
   951→            try {
   952→                val response = httpClient.toBlocking().exchange(request, Map::class.java)
   953→                pageCount++
   954→
   955→                when (response.status.code) {
   956→                    200 -> {
   957→                        @Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
   958→                        val responseBody = response.body() as? Map<String, Any>
   959→                            ?: throw CrowdStrikeException("Empty response from Spotlight API")
   960→
   961→                        val resources = responseBody["resources"] as? List<*> ?: emptyList<Any>()
   962→                        val vulns = mapResponseToDtos(resources, metadataByDeviceId)
   963→
   964→                        val filtered = vulns.filter { vuln ->
   965→                            val daysOpenValue = vuln.daysOpen?.split(" ")?.firstOrNull()?.toIntOrNull() ?: 0
   966→                            daysOpenValue >= minDaysOpen
   967→                        }
   968→
   969→                        val meta = responseBody["meta"] as? Map<*, *>
   970→                        val pagination = meta?.get("pagination") as? Map<*, *>
   971→                        val prevAfterToken = afterToken
   972→                        afterToken = pagination?.get("after")?.toString()
   973→
   974→                        log.info(">>> Batch {}/{} page {}: Retrieved {} vulns, {} after filter (batch total: {})",
   975→                            batchIndex + 1, totalBatches, pageCount, vulns.size, filtered.size,
   976→                            batchVulnerabilities.size + filtered.size)
   977→                        log.debug(">>> Batch {}/{} page {}: Pagination - afterToken: {}, vulns.isNotEmpty: {}",
   978→                            batchIndex + 1, totalBatches, pageCount,
   979→                            if (afterToken != null) "present" else "null",
   980→                            vulns.isNotEmpty())
   981→
   982→                        batchVulnerabilities.addAll(filtered)
   983→
   984→                        hasMore = afterToken != null && vulns.isNotEmpty()
   985→
   986→                        if (hasMore && afterToken == prevAfterToken) {
   987→                            log.warn(">>> Batch {}/{} page {}: afterToken unchanged - breaking pagination loop",
   988→                                batchIndex + 1, totalBatches, pageCount)
   989→                            hasMore = false
   990→                        }
   991→
   992→                        if (hasMore && filtered.isEmpty() && vulns.isNotEmpty() && pageCount >= 3) {
   993→                            log.warn(">>> Batch {}/{} page {}: Got {} vulns but ALL filtered out for {} pages - stopping pagination (minDaysOpen={} may be too strict)",
   994→                                batchIndex + 1, totalBatches, pageCount, vulns.size, pageCount, minDaysOpen)
   995→                            hasMore = false
   996→                        }
   997→                    }
   998→                    404 -> hasMore = false
   999→                    429 -> {
  1000→                        val retryAfter = response.headers.get("Retry-After")?.toLongOrNull() ?: 30L
  1001→                        throw RateLimitException("Rate limit exceeded on batch ${batchIndex + 1}", retryAfter)
  1002→                    }
  1003→                    in 500..599 -> throw CrowdStrikeException("Spotlight API server error: ${response.status}")
  1004→                    else -> throw CrowdStrikeException("Unexpected Spotlight API response: ${response.status}")
  1005→                }
  1006→            } catch (e: io.micronaut.http.client.exceptions.HttpClientResponseException) {
  1007→                when (e.status.code) {
  1008→                    401 -> {
  1009→                        log.warn(">>> Batch {}/{}: Unauthorized - refreshing token (expires at: {})",
  1010→                            batchIndex + 1, totalBatches, token.expiresAt)
  1011→                        authService.clearCache()
  1012→                        token = getAuthToken(config)
  1013→                        continue
  1014→                    }
  1015→                    404 -> hasMore = false
  1016→                    429 -> {
  1017→                        val retryAfter = e.response.headers.get("Retry-After")?.toLongOrNull() ?: 30L
  1018→                        log.warn(">>> Batch {}/{}: Rate limit, retrying after {}s",
  1019→                            batchIndex + 1, totalBatches, retryAfter)
  1020→                        throw RateLimitException("Rate limit exceeded", retryAfter, e)
  1021→                    }
  1022→                    in 500..599 -> {
  1023→                        // Server errors are often transient - retry with backoff
  1024→                        if (retryTransientError(batchIndex, totalBatches, pageCount, "Server error ${e.status.code}", e)) {
  1025→                            continue
  1026→                        }
  1027→                        throw CrowdStrikeException("Server error: ${e.status}", e)
  1028→                    }
  1029→                    else -> {
  1030→                        log.error(">>> Batch {}/{}: API error {}", batchIndex + 1, totalBatches, e.status)
  1031→                        throw CrowdStrikeException("API error: ${e.message}", e)
  1032→                    }
  1033→                }
  1034→            } catch (e: java.net.SocketTimeoutException) {
  1035→                // Retry transient network errors with backoff
  1036→                if (retryTransientError(batchIndex, totalBatches, pageCount, "Timeout", e)) {
  1037→                    continue
  1038→                }
  1039→                throw CrowdStrikeException("Timeout waiting for CrowdStrike API response on batch ${batchIndex + 1}", e)
  1040→            } catch (e: java.io.IOException) {
  1041→                if (retryTransientError(batchIndex, totalBatches, pageCount, "Network I/O error", e)) {
  1042→                    continue
  1043→                }
  1044→                throw CrowdStrikeException("Network error on batch ${batchIndex + 1}: ${e.message}", e)
  1045→            } catch (e: io.netty.channel.ChannelException) {
  1046→                if (retryTransientError(batchIndex, totalBatches, pageCount, "Channel error", e)) {
  1047→                    continue
  1048→                }
  1049→                throw CrowdStrikeException("Channel error during batch ${batchIndex + 1}: ${e.message}", e)

I need to understand the full CrowdStrike vulnerability import pipeline from the CLI side, focusing on memory characteristics. Please trace the entire flow:

1. Find the CLI command that queries CrowdStrike and sends data to the backend. Look in `src/cli/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/cli/commands/` for CrowdStrike-related commands.
2. Trace how vulnerabilities are fetched from CrowdStrike API — look at `src/cli/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/cli/service/` for CrowdStrike service classes.
3. Find how the CLI batches/sends vulnerability data to the backend API endpoint.
4. Look at the DTOs used for the import (CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityBatchDto and related).

Focus on:
- How many vulnerabilities are held in memory at once
- Whether there's any streaming or batching
- What data structures accumulate in memory
- The HTTP payload size and structure
- Any pagination from the CrowdStrike API

Be very thorough — read complete files, not just snippets.

1→package com.secman.service
     2→
     3→import com.secman.domain.Asset
     4→import com.secman.domain.Vulnerability
     5→import com.secman.dto.*
     6→import com.secman.repository.AssetRepository
     7→import com.secman.repository.FalconConfigRepository
     8→import com.secman.repository.UserRepository
     9→import com.secman.repository.VulnerabilityExceptionRepository
    10→import com.secman.repository.VulnerabilityRepository
    11→import io.micronaut.http.HttpRequest
    12→import io.micronaut.http.HttpResponse
    13→import io.micronaut.http.client.HttpClient
    14→import io.micronaut.http.client.annotation.Client
    15→import io.micronaut.http.uri.UriBuilder
    16→import io.micronaut.retry.annotation.Retryable
    17→import io.micronaut.security.authentication.Authentication
    18→import jakarta.inject.Singleton
    19→import jakarta.persistence.EntityManager
    20→import jakarta.persistence.LockModeType
    21→import jakarta.transaction.Transactional
    22→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
    23→import java.time.LocalDateTime
    24→import java.time.temporal.ChronoUnit
    25→
    26→/**
    27→ * Service for integrating with CrowdStrike Falcon API
    28→ *
    29→ * Provides functionality to:
    30→ * - Query CrowdStrike for system vulnerabilities (last 40 days, OPEN status)
    31→ * - Authenticate with OAuth2 client credentials flow
    32→ * - Cache OAuth2 tokens (30 minutes)
    33→ * - Map CrowdStrike response to internal DTOs
    34→ * - Check against VulnerabilityException table
    35→ * - Save vulnerabilities to database
    36→ *
    37→ * Related to: Feature 015-we-have-currently (CrowdStrike System Vulnerability Lookup)
    38→ * Tasks: T018-T026 [US1-Impl], T049-T050 [US3-Impl]
    39→ */
    40→@Singleton
    41→open class CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityService(
    42→    @Client("\${secman.http.services.crowdstrike.base-urls.us-1}")
    43→    private val crowdStrikeClient: HttpClient,
    44→    private val falconConfigRepository: FalconConfigRepository,
    45→    private val assetRepository: AssetRepository,
    46→    private val vulnerabilityRepository: VulnerabilityRepository,
    47→    private val vulnerabilityExceptionRepository: VulnerabilityExceptionRepository,
    48→    private val userRepository: UserRepository,
    49→    private val entityManager: EntityManager
    50→) {
    51→    private val log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityService::class.java)
    52→
    53→    // OAuth2 token cache
    54→    private var cachedToken: String? = null
    55→    private var tokenExpiresAt: LocalDateTime? = null
    56→
    57→    // FalconConfig cache (avoids repeated DB queries)
    58→    private var cachedConfig: com.secman.domain.FalconConfig? = null
    59→
    60→    /**
    61→     * Query CrowdStrike for vulnerabilities by hostname
    62→     *
    63→     * Task: T026 [US1-Impl]
    64→     *
    65→     * @param hostname System hostname to query
    66→     * @return CrowdStrikeQueryResponse with vulnerabilities
    67→     * @throws CrowdStrikeError.ConfigurationError if credentials not configured
    68→     * @throws CrowdStrikeError.AuthenticationError if OAuth2 fails
    69→     * @throws CrowdStrikeError.NotFoundError if hostname not found
    70→     * @throws CrowdStrikeError.RateLimitError if rate limit exceeded
    71→     * @throws CrowdStrikeError.NetworkError if network issues
    72→     * @throws CrowdStrikeError.ServerError if CrowdStrike service unavailable
    73→     */
    74→    fun queryByHostname(hostname: String): CrowdStrikeQueryResponse {
    75→        require(hostname.isNotBlank()) { "Hostname cannot be blank" }
    76→
    77→        log.info("Querying CrowdStrike for vulnerabilities: hostname={}", hostname)
    78→
    79→        try {
    80→            // 1. Authenticate
    81→            val token = authenticateWithCrowdStrike()
    82→
    83→            // 2. Query Spotlight API
    84→            val apiResponse = queryCrowdStrikeApi(hostname, token)
    85→
    86→            // 3. Map to DTOs
    87→            val vulnerabilityDtos = mapToDtos(apiResponse)
    88→
    89→            // 4. Check exceptions
    90→            val vulnsWithExceptions = checkExceptions(vulnerabilityDtos)
    91→
    92→            log.info("Successfully queried CrowdStrike: hostname={}, count={}", hostname, vulnsWithExceptions.size)
    93→
    94→            return CrowdStrikeQueryResponse(
    95→                hostname = hostname,
    96→                vulnerabilities = vulnsWithExceptions,
    97→                totalCount = vulnsWithExceptions.size,
    98→                queriedAt = LocalDateTime.now()
    99→            )
   100→        } catch (e: CrowdStrikeError) {
   101→            log.error("CrowdStrike query failed: hostname={}, error={}", hostname, e.message)
   102→            throw e
   103→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   104→            log.error("Unexpected error querying CrowdStrike: hostname={}", hostname, e)
   105→            throw CrowdStrikeError.ServerError(message = "Unexpected error: ${e.message}", cause = e)
   106→        }
   107→    }
   108→
   109→    /**
   110→     * Load CrowdStrike configuration from database
   111→     *
   112→     * Task: T019 [US1-Impl]
   113→     *
   114→     * @return FalconConfig with credentials
   115→     * @throws CrowdStrikeError.ConfigurationError if no config found
   116→     */
   117→    private fun loadFalconConfig(): com.secman.domain.FalconConfig {
   118→        // Return cached config if available
   119→        if (cachedConfig != null) {
   120→            return cachedConfig!!
   121→        }
   122→
   123→        log.debug("Loading FalconConfig from database")
   124→
   125→        // Try to find active config
   126→        val activeConfig = falconConfigRepository.findActiveConfig()
   127→        if (activeConfig.isPresent) {
   128→            log.debug("Found active FalconConfig: id={}", activeConfig.get().id)
   129→            cachedConfig = activeConfig.get()
   130→            return activeConfig.get()
   131→        }
   132→
   133→        // Fallback: Find most recent config
   134→        val recentConfig = falconConfigRepository.findMostRecent()
   135→        if (recentConfig.isPresent) {
   136→            log.warn("No active FalconConfig found, using most recent: id={}", recentConfig.get().id)
   137→            cachedConfig = recentConfig.get()
   138→            return recentConfig.get()
   139→        }
   140→
   141→        // No config found
   142→        log.error("No FalconConfig found in database")
   143→        throw CrowdStrikeError.ConfigurationError("CrowdStrike API credentials not configured in database. Contact administrator.")
   144→    }
   145→
   146→    /**
   147→     * Authenticate with CrowdStrike using OAuth2 client credentials
   148→     *
   149→     * Task: T020 [US1-Impl]
   150→     *
   151→     * @return OAuth2 access token
   152→     * @throws CrowdStrikeError.ConfigurationError if credentials not configured
   153→     * @throws CrowdStrikeError.AuthenticationError if auth fails
   154→     */
   155→    private fun authenticateWithCrowdStrike(): String {
   156→        // Check if cached token is still valid
   157→        if (cachedToken != null && tokenExpiresAt != null && LocalDateTime.now().isBefore(tokenExpiresAt)) {
   158→            log.debug("Using cached OAuth2 token")
   159→            return cachedToken!!
   160→        }
   161→
   162→        log.debug("Authenticating with CrowdStrike (OAuth2 client credentials)")
   163→
   164→        // Load credentials from database
   165→        val config = loadFalconConfig()
   166→
   167→        try {
   168→            // Build OAuth2 token request
   169→			val baseUrl ="https://api.crowdstrike.com"
   170→            val tokenRequest = HttpRequest.POST(baseUrl + "/oauth2/token", mapOf(
   171→                "client_id" to config.clientId,
   172→                "client_secret" to config.clientSecret
   173→            ))
   174→                .contentType(io.micronaut.http.MediaType.APPLICATION_FORM_URLENCODED)
   175→
   176→            // Make OAuth2 request
   177→            val response = crowdStrikeClient.toBlocking().exchange(tokenRequest, Map::class.java)
   178→
   179→            if (response.status.code != 201) {
   180→                // SECURITY: Never log credentials - only log client_id prefix for debugging
   181→                log.error("OAuth2 authentication failed: status={}, code={}, client_id_prefix={}",
   182→                    response.status, response.status.code,
   183→                    config.clientId.take(4) + "****")
   184→
   185→                throw CrowdStrikeError.AuthenticationError("CrowdStrike authentication failed with status ${response.status}")
   186→            }
   187→
   188→            val tokenData = response.body() as Map<*, *>
   189→            val accessToken = tokenData["access_token"]?.toString()
   190→                ?: throw CrowdStrikeError.AuthenticationError("No access_token in response")
   191→
   192→            val expiresIn = (tokenData["expires_in"] as? Number)?.toLong() ?: 1800L
   193→
   194→            // Cache token (subtract 60 seconds for safety margin)
   195→            cachedToken = accessToken
   196→            tokenExpiresAt = LocalDateTime.now().plusSeconds(expiresIn - 60)
   197→
   198→            log.info("Successfully authenticated with CrowdStrike, token expires at {}", tokenExpiresAt)
   199→            return accessToken
   200→        } catch (e: io.micronaut.http.client.exceptions.HttpClientResponseException) {
   201→            log.error("HTTP error during authentication: status={}", e.status, e)
   202→            if (e.status.code == 401 || e.status.code == 403) {
   203→                throw CrowdStrikeError.AuthenticationError("Invalid CrowdStrike credentials", e)
   204→            }
   205→            throw CrowdStrikeError.AuthenticationError("Authentication failed: ${e.message}", e)
   206→        } catch (e: CrowdStrikeError) {
   207→            throw e
   208→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   209→            log.error("Unexpected error during authentication", e)
   210→            throw CrowdStrikeError.AuthenticationError("Authentication error: ${e.message}", e)
   211→        }
   212→    }
   213→
   214→    /**
   215→     * Get device ID (aid) by hostname
   216→     *
   217→     * Task: T021-FIX [Bug fix - hostname to device ID lookup]
   218→     *
   219→     * CrowdStrike Spotlight API doesn't support filtering by hostname directly.
   220→     * Must first query Hosts API to get the device ID (aid).
   221→     *
   222→     * Uses multi-strategy approach with fallback:
   223→     * 1. Stemmed search (case-insensitive, starts with): hostname:'value*'
   224→     * 2. Contains search (case-insensitive, anywhere): hostname:*'*value*'
   225→     * 3. Exact match (case-sensitive): hostname:['value']
   226→     * 4. Fallback: Get all devices and filter locally (case-insensitive)
   227→     *
   228→     * @param hostname System hostname
   229→     * @param token OAuth2 access token
   230→     * @return Device ID (aid) or null if not found
   231→     * @throws CrowdStrikeError.NetworkError if network issues
   232→     * @throws CrowdStrikeError.ServerError if server error (500+)
   233→     */
   234→    private fun getDeviceIdByHostname(hostname: String, token: String): String? {
   235→        log.info("Looking up device ID for hostname: {}", hostname)
   236→
   237→        // Strategy 1: Stemmed search (case-insensitive, starts with) - RECOMMENDED by CrowdStrike
   238→        log.debug("Strategy 1: Trying stemmed search (starts with, case-insensitive)")
   239→        val stemmedResult = tryHostnameFilter(hostname, "hostname:'$hostname*'", token)
   240→        if (stemmedResult != null) {
   241→            log.info("Found device ID using stemmed search: {}", stemmedResult)
   242→            return stemmedResult
   243→        }
   244→
   245→        // Strategy 2: Contains search (case-insensitive, anywhere in hostname)
   246→        log.debug("Strategy 2: Trying contains search (anywhere, case-insensitive)")
   247→        val containsResult = tryHostnameFilter(hostname, "hostname:*'*$hostname*'", token)
   248→        if (containsResult != null) {
   249→            log.info("Found device ID using contains search: {}", containsResult)
   250→            return containsResult
   251→        }
   252→
   253→        // Strategy 3: Exact match (case-sensitive with square brackets)
   254→        log.debug("Strategy 3: Trying exact match (case-sensitive)")
   255→        val exactResult = tryHostnameFilter(hostname, "hostname:['$hostname']", token)
   256→        if (exactResult != null) {
   257→            log.info("Found device ID using exact match: {}", exactResult)
   258→            return exactResult
   259→        }
   260→
   261→        // Strategy 4: Fallback - Get all devices and filter locally (case-insensitive)
   262→        log.warn("All filter strategies failed. Attempting fallback: get all devices and filter locally")
   263→        val fallbackResult = getAllDevicesAndFilterByHostname(hostname, token)
   264→        if (fallbackResult != null) {
   265→            log.info("Found device ID using local filtering: {}", fallbackResult)
   266→            return fallbackResult
   267→        }
   268→
   269→        log.warn("All strategies exhausted. No device found for hostname: {}", hostname)
   270→        return null
   271→    }
   272→
   273→    /**
   274→     * Try a single hostname filter strategy
   275→     *
   276→     * @param hostname Original hostname (for logging)
   277→     * @param filter FQL filter string
   278→     * @param token OAuth2 access token
   279→     * @return Device ID or null if not found
   280→     */
   281→    @Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
   282→    private fun tryHostnameFilter(hostname: String, filter: String, token: String): String? {
   283→        try {
   284→            // Build request URL with proper encoding
   285→            val baseUrl = "https://api.crowdstrike.com"
   286→            val uri = UriBuilder.of("/devices/queries/devices/v1")
   287→                .queryParam("filter", filter)
   288→                .queryParam("limit", "10")  // Get up to 10 matches
   289→                .build()
   290→
   291→            val fullUrl = baseUrl + uri.toString()
   292→            log.debug("Trying filter: {} | Full URL: {}", filter, fullUrl)
   293→
   294→            val request = HttpRequest.GET<Any>(fullUrl)
   295→                .header("Authorization", "Bearer $token")
   296→                .header("Accept", "application/json")
   297→
   298→            // Make API request
   299→            val response = crowdStrikeClient.toBlocking().exchange(request, Map::class.java)
   300→
   301→            when (response.status.code) {
   302→                200 -> {
   303→                    val responseBody = response.body() as? Map<String, Any>
   304→                        ?: throw CrowdStrikeError.ServerError(message = "Empty response from CrowdStrike Hosts API")
   305→
   306→                    // Extract device IDs from response
   307→                    val resources = responseBody["resources"] as? List<*>
   308→                    if (resources.isNullOrEmpty()) {
   309→                        log.debug("Filter returned 0 results: {}", filter)
   310→                        return null
   311→                    }
   312→
   313→                    val deviceId = resources[0]?.toString()
   314→                    log.debug("Filter returned {} results. Using first device ID: {}", resources.size, deviceId)
   315→                    return deviceId
   316→                }
   317→                429 -> {
   318→                    val retryAfter = response.headers.get("Retry-After")?.toIntOrNull() ?: 30
   319→                    log.warn("CrowdStrike rate limit exceeded during device lookup, retry after {} seconds", retryAfter)
   320→                    throw CrowdStrikeError.RateLimitError(retryAfter)
   321→                }
   322→                in 500..599 -> {
   323→                    log.error("CrowdStrike server error during device lookup: status={}", response.status)
   324→                    throw CrowdStrikeError.ServerError(response.status.code)
   325→                }
   326→                else -> {
   327→                    log.error("Unexpected CrowdStrike response during device lookup: status={}", response.status)
   328→                    throw CrowdStrikeError.ServerError(
   329→                        statusCode = response.status.code,
   330→                        message = "Unexpected response: ${response.status}"
   331→                    )
   332→                }
   333→            }
   334→        } catch (e: io.micronaut.http.client.exceptions.HttpClientResponseException) {
   335→            when (e.status.code) {
   336→                429 -> {
   337→                    val retryAfter = e.response.headers.get("Retry-After")?.toIntOrNull() ?: 30
   338→                    throw CrowdStrikeError.RateLimitError(retryAfter, cause = e)
   339→                }
   340→                in 500..599 -> throw CrowdStrikeError.ServerError(e.status.code, cause = e)
   341→                else -> {
   342→                    log.warn("Filter failed with status {}: {}", e.status.code, filter)
   343→                    return null  // Don't throw, try next strategy
   344→                }
   345→            }
   346→        } catch (e: CrowdStrikeError) {
   347→            throw e
   348→        } catch (e: java.net.SocketTimeoutException) {
   349→            log.error("Network timeout querying CrowdStrike Hosts API", e)
   350→            throw CrowdStrikeError.NetworkError(cause = e)
   351→        } catch (e: java.io.IOException) {
   352→            log.error("Network error querying CrowdStrike Hosts API", e)
   353→            throw CrowdStrikeError.NetworkError(cause = e)
   354→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   355→            log.error("Unexpected error querying CrowdStrike Hosts API", e)
   356→            return null  // Don't throw, try next strategy
   357→        }
   358→    }

   415→    /**
   416→     * Query CrowdStrike Spotlight API for vulnerabilities
   417→     *
   418→     * Task: T021 [US1-Impl] - Updated to use device ID instead of hostname
   419→     *
   420→     * @param hostname System hostname
   421→     * @param token OAuth2 access token
   422→     * @return Raw API response JSON
   423→     * @throws CrowdStrikeError.NotFoundError if hostname not found (404)
   424→     * @throws CrowdStrikeError.RateLimitError if rate limit exceeded (429)
   425→     * @throws CrowdStrikeError.ServerError if server error (500+)
   426→     * @throws CrowdStrikeError.NetworkError if network issues
   427→     */
   428→    @Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
   429→    @Retryable(includes = [CrowdStrikeError.RateLimitError::class], attempts = "3", delay = "1s", multiplier = "2.0")
   430→    open fun queryCrowdStrikeApi(hostname: String, token: String): Map<String, Any> {
   431→        log.debug("Querying CrowdStrike Spotlight API: hostname={}", hostname)
   432→
   433→        // Step 1: Get device ID from hostname (declare outside try block for catch block access)
   434→        val deviceId = try {
   435→            val id = getDeviceIdByHostname(hostname, token)
   436→            if (id == null) {
   437→                log.warn("Core error, initial calls failed... Hostname not found in CrowdStrike: {}", hostname)
   438→                log.warn("Hostname not found in CrowdStrike: {}", hostname)
   439→                throw CrowdStrikeError.NotFoundError(hostname)
   440→            }
   441→            id
   442→        } catch (e: CrowdStrikeError) {
   443→            throw e
   444→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   445→            log.error("Failed to lookup device ID for hostname: {}", hostname, e)
   446→            throw CrowdStrikeError.ServerError(message = "Device lookup failed: ${e.message}", cause = e)
   447→        }
   448→
   449→        log.info("Using device ID '{}' for hostname '{}'", deviceId, hostname)
   450→
   451→        try {
   452→
   453→            // Calculate 40 days ago timestamp in ISO 8601 format
   454→            val fortyDaysAgo = LocalDateTime.now().minusDays(40)
   455→            // Format: 2025-09-02T22:10:32Z (ISO 8601 with 'T' separator and 'Z' suffix)
   456→            // Truncate nanoseconds to seconds for better API compatibility
   457→            val timestampFilter = fortyDaysAgo.toString().substring(0, 19) + "Z"
   458→
   459→            log.debug("Using timestamp filter: {}", timestampFilter)
   460→
   461→            // Build filter query: aid:'<device_id>'+status:'open'
   462→            val filter = "aid:'$deviceId'+status:'open'"
   463→
   464→            // Build request URL - Using v1 endpoint (documented version)
   465→            val baseUrl = "https://api.crowdstrike.com"
   466→            val uri = UriBuilder.of("/spotlight/combined/vulnerabilities/v1")
   467→                .queryParam("filter", filter)
   468→                .queryParam("limit", "5000")  // Max results per CrowdStrike docs
   469→                .queryParam("facet", "cve")  // Include detailed CVE information with CVSS and severity
   470→                .build()
   471→
   472→            val fullUrl = baseUrl + uri.toString()
   473→            log.debug("CrowdStrike Spotlight API request: url={}, filter={}, facet=cve", fullUrl, filter)
   474→
   475→            val request = HttpRequest.GET<Any>(fullUrl)
   476→                .header("Authorization", "Bearer $token")
   477→                .header("Accept", "application/json")
   478→
   479→            // Make API request
   480→            val response = crowdStrikeClient.toBlocking().exchange(request, Map::class.java)
   481→
   482→            when (response.status.code) {
   483→                200 -> {
   484→                    val responseBody = response.body() as? Map<String, Any>
   485→                        ?: throw CrowdStrikeError.ServerError(message = "Empty response from CrowdStrike")
   486→
   487→                    val resources = responseBody["resources"] as? List<*>
   488→                    val resourceCount = resources?.size ?: 0
   489→                    log.info("CrowdStrike Spotlight API response: status=200, vulnerabilities found={}", resourceCount)
   490→
   491→                    if (resourceCount == 0) {
   492→                        log.info("Device '{}' (aid: {}) has no open vulnerabilities", hostname, deviceId)
   493→                    }
   494→
   495→                    return responseBody
   496→                }
   497→                404 -> {
   498→                    // 404 from Spotlight API typically means no vulnerabilities found (not device not found)
   499→                    // Device lookup already succeeded, so treat this as "no vulnerabilities"
   500→                    log.info("Spotlight API returned 404 for device '{}' (aid: {}). Treating as no vulnerabilities.", hostname, deviceId)
   501→
   502→                    // Return empty response structure
   503→                    return mapOf(
   504→                        "resources" to emptyList<Any>(),
   505→                        "meta" to mapOf("total" to 0)
   506→                    )
   507→                }
   508→                429 -> {
   509→                    val retryAfter = response.headers.get("Retry-After")?.toIntOrNull() ?: 30
   510→                    log.warn("CrowdStrike rate limit exceeded, retry after {} seconds", retryAfter)
   511→                    throw CrowdStrikeError.RateLimitError(retryAfter)
   512→                }
   513→                in 500..599 -> {
   514→                    log.error("CrowdStrike server error: status={}", response.status)
   515→                    throw CrowdStrikeError.ServerError(response.status.code)
   516→                }
   517→                else -> {
   518→                    log.error("Unexpected CrowdStrike response: status={}", response.status)
   519→                    throw CrowdStrikeError.ServerError(
   520→                        statusCode = response.status.code,
   521→                        message = "Unexpected response: ${response.status}"
   522→                    )
   523→                }
   524→            }
   525→        } catch (e: io.micronaut.http.client.exceptions.HttpClientResponseException) {
   526→            when (e.status.code) {
   527→                404 -> {
   528→                    // 404 from Spotlight API - device has no vulnerabilities
   529→                    log.info("Spotlight API returned 404 for device '{}' (aid: {}). Response: {}", hostname, deviceId, e.response.body.orElse(null))
   530→
   531→                    // Return empty response structure instead of throwing error
   532→                    return mapOf(
   533→                        "resources" to emptyList<Any>(),
   534→                        "meta" to mapOf("total" to 0)
   535→                    )
   536→                }
   537→                429 -> {
   538→                    val retryAfter = e.response.headers.get("Retry-After")?.toIntOrNull() ?: 30
   539→                    throw CrowdStrikeError.RateLimitError(retryAfter, cause = e)
   540→                }
   541→                in 500..599 -> throw CrowdStrikeError.ServerError(e.status.code, cause = e)
   542→                else -> {
   543→                    log.error("Spotlight API error: status={}, message={}, response={}", e.status.code, e.message, e.response.body.orElse(null))
   544→                    throw CrowdStrikeError.ServerError(e.status.code, "API error: ${e.message}", e)
   545→                }
   546→            }
   547→        } catch (e: CrowdStrikeError) {
   548→            throw e
   549→        } catch (e: java.net.SocketTimeoutException) {
   550→            log.error("Network timeout querying CrowdStrike", e)
   551→            throw CrowdStrikeError.NetworkError(cause = e)
   552→        } catch (e: java.io.IOException) {
   553→            log.error("Network error querying CrowdStrike", e)
   554→            throw CrowdStrikeError.NetworkError(cause = e)
   555→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   556→            log.error("Unexpected error querying CrowdStrike API", e)
   557→            throw CrowdStrikeError.ServerError(message = "Unexpected error: ${e.message}", cause = e)
   558→        }
   559→    }

   561→    /**
   562→     * Map CVSS score to severity level
   563→     *
   564→     * Task: T022 [US1-Impl]
   565→     *
   566→     * @param score CVSS score (0.0-10.0)
   567→     * @return Severity level (Critical, High, Medium, Low, Unknown)
   568→     */
   569→    private fun mapCvssScoreToSeverity(score: Double?): String {
   570→        return when {
   571→            score == null -> "Unknown"
   572→            score >= 9.0 -> "Critical"
   573→            score >= 7.0 -> "High"
   574→            score >= 4.0 -> "Medium"
   575→            score >= 0.1 -> "Low"
   576→            else -> "Unknown"
   577→        }
   578→    }
   579→
   580→    /**
   581→     * Normalize severity string from CrowdStrike API
   582→     * Handles various formats: CRITICAL, critical, Critical, etc.
   583→     *
   584→     * @param apiSeverity Raw severity string from API
   585→     * @return Normalized severity (Critical, High, Medium, Low, Informational, Unknown)
   586→     */
   587→    private fun normalizeSeverity(apiSeverity: String): String {
   588→        return when (apiSeverity.uppercase()) {
   589→            "CRITICAL" -> "Critical"
   590→            "HIGH" -> "High"
   591→            "MEDIUM", "MODERATE" -> "Medium"
   592→            "LOW" -> "Low"
   593→            "INFORMATIONAL", "INFO" -> "Informational"
   594→            else -> apiSeverity.replaceFirstChar { it.uppercase() } // Capitalize first letter
   595→        }
   596→    }
   597→
   598→    /**
   599→     * Parse detection timestamp from CrowdStrike vulnerability data
   600→     * Handles multiple timestamp formats and field names
   601→     *
   602→     * @param vuln Vulnerability map from CrowdStrike API
   603→     * @return Parsed LocalDateTime
   604→     */
   605→    private fun parseDetectionTimestamp(vuln: Map<*, *>): LocalDateTime {
   606→        // Try different field names
   607→        val timestampValue = vuln["created_timestamp"]
   608→            ?: vuln["created_on"]
   609→            ?: vuln["cve_created_timestamp"]
   610→            ?: vuln["first_found_date"]
   611→            ?: vuln["remediation"]?.let { (it as? Map<*, *>)?.get("created_timestamp") }
   612→
   613→        if (timestampValue == null) {
   614→            log.warn("No timestamp field found in vulnerability, using current time")
   615→            return LocalDateTime.now()
   616→        }
   617→
   618→        // Try to parse as epoch milliseconds (number)
   619→        if (timestampValue is Number) {
   620→            return try {
   621→                val epochSeconds = timestampValue.toLong()
   622→                // Handle both seconds and milliseconds
   623→                val instant = if (epochSeconds > 10000000000L) {
   624→                    java.time.Instant.ofEpochMilli(epochSeconds)
   625→                } else {
   626→                    java.time.Instant.ofEpochSecond(epochSeconds)
   627→                }
   628→                LocalDateTime.ofInstant(instant, java.time.ZoneId.systemDefault())
   629→            } catch (e: Exception) {
   630→                log.warn("Failed to parse epoch timestamp: {}", timestampValue, e)
   631→                LocalDateTime.now()
   632→            }
   633→        }
   634→
   635→        // Try to parse as ISO string
   636→        val timestampString = timestampValue.toString()
   637→        return try {
   638→            // Try ISO format with various patterns
   639→            when {
   640→                timestampString.contains("T") -> LocalDateTime.parse(timestampString.substringBefore(".").substringBefore("Z"))
   641→                timestampString.contains(" ") -> LocalDateTime.parse(timestampString.replace(" ", "T").substringBefore("."))
   642→                else -> {
   643→                    log.warn("Unknown timestamp format: {}", timestampString)
   644→                    LocalDateTime.now()
   645→                }
   646→            }
   647→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   648→            log.warn("Failed to parse timestamp string: {}", timestampString, e)
   649→            LocalDateTime.now()
   650→        }
   651→    }
   652→
   653→    /**
   654→     * Calculate days open since detection
   655→     *
   656→     * Task: T023 [US1-Impl]
   657→     *
   658→     * @param detectedAt Detection timestamp
   659→     * @return Days open string (e.g., "15 days")
   660→     */
   661→    private fun calculateDaysOpen(detectedAt: LocalDateTime): String {
   662→        val days = ChronoUnit.DAYS.between(detectedAt, LocalDateTime.now())
   663→        return if (days == 1L) "1 day" else "$days days"
   664→    }
   665→
   666→    /**
   667→     * Map CrowdStrike API response to DTOs
   668→     *
   669→     * Task: T024 [US1-Impl]
   670→     *
   671→     * @param apiResponse Raw API response
   672→     * @return List of CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityDto
   673→     */
   674→    private fun mapToDtos(apiResponse: Map<String, Any>): List<CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityDto> {
   675→        log.debug("Mapping CrowdStrike API response to DTOs")
   676→
   677→        try {
   678→            // Extract resources array from response
   679→            val resources = apiResponse["resources"] as? List<*> ?: emptyList<Any>()
   680→
   681→            if (resources.isEmpty()) {
   682→                log.debug("No vulnerabilities found in CrowdStrike response")
   683→                return emptyList()
   684→            }
   685→
   686→            log.debug("Mapping {} vulnerabilities from CrowdStrike response", resources.size)
   687→
   688→            return resources.mapNotNull { resource ->
   689→                try {
   690→                    val vuln = resource as? Map<*, *> ?: return@mapNotNull null
   691→
   692→                    // DEBUG: Log all available fields in vulnerability
   693→                    log.debug("CrowdStrike vulnerability fields: {}", vuln.keys.joinToString(", "))
   694→
   695→                    // Extract fields from CrowdStrike response
   696→                    val id = vuln["id"]?.toString() ?: "cs-${System.currentTimeMillis()}"
   697→                    val hostInfo = vuln["host_info"] as? Map<*, *>
   698→                    val deviceInfo = vuln["device"] as? Map<*, *>
   699→
   700→                    // Prefer real host metadata before falling back to the CrowdStrike agent ID
   701→                    val hostname = vuln["hostname"]?.toString()
   702→                        ?: hostInfo?.get("hostname")?.toString()
   703→                        ?: hostInfo?.get("host_name")?.toString()
   704→                        ?: deviceInfo?.get("hostname")?.toString()
   705→                        ?: deviceInfo?.get("host_name")?.toString()
   706→                        ?: vuln["aid"]?.toString()
   707→                        ?: ""
   708→
   709→                    val ip = vuln["local_ip"]?.toString()
   710→                        ?: hostInfo?.get("local_ip")?.toString()
   711→                        ?: deviceInfo?.get("local_ip")?.toString()
   712→
   713→                    // CVE information
   714→                    val cve = vuln["cve"] as? Map<*, *>
   715→                    val cveId = cve?.get("id")?.toString()
   716→
   717→                    // DEBUG: Log CVE fields if present
   718→                    if (cve != null) {
   719→                        log.debug("CVE fields: {}", cve.keys.joinToString(", "))
   720→                    }
   721→
   722→                    // CVSS score and severity - try multiple field locations
   723→                    // CrowdStrike Spotlight API can return severity in different fields
   724→                    val cvssScore = (vuln["score"] as? Number)?.toDouble()
   725→                        ?: (cve?.get("base_score") as? Number)?.toDouble()
   726→                        ?: (vuln["base_score"] as? Number)?.toDouble()
   727→
   728→                    // Try to get severity directly from API, otherwise map from CVSS score
   729→                    val severityFromApi = vuln["severity"]?.toString()
   730→                        ?: cve?.get("severity")?.toString()
   731→
   732→                    val severity = when {
   733→                        severityFromApi != null -> normalizeSeverity(severityFromApi)
   734→                        cvssScore != null -> mapCvssScoreToSeverity(cvssScore)
   735→                        else -> "Unknown"
   736→                    }
   737→
   738→                    log.debug("Vulnerability {} - severity: {}, cvssScore: {}, severityFromApi: {}",
   739→                        cveId ?: id, severity, cvssScore, severityFromApi)
   740→
   741→                    // Affected product
   742→                    val apps = vuln["apps"] as? List<*>
   743→                    val affectedProduct = apps?.mapNotNull { app ->
   744→                        val appMap = app as? Map<*, *>
   745→                        appMap?.get("product_name_version")?.toString()
   746→                    }?.joinToString(", ")
   747→
   748→                    // Detection timestamp - try multiple field names and formats
   749→                    val detectedAt = parseDetectionTimestamp(vuln)
   750→
   751→                    // Days open
   752→                    val daysOpen = calculateDaysOpen(detectedAt)
   753→
   754→                    log.debug("Vulnerability {} - detectedAt: {}, daysOpen: {}",
   755→                        cveId ?: id, detectedAt, daysOpen)
   756→
   757→                    // Status
   758→                    val status = vuln["status"]?.toString() ?: "open"
   759→
   760→                    CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityDto(
   761→                        id = id,
   762→                        hostname = hostname,
   763→                        ip = ip,
   764→                        cveId = cveId,
   765→                        severity = severity,
   766→                        cvssScore = cvssScore,
   767→                        affectedProduct = affectedProduct,
   768→                        daysOpen = daysOpen,
   769→                        detectedAt = detectedAt,
   770→                        status = status,
   771→                        hasException = false,  // Will be updated by checkExceptions()
   772→                        exceptionReason = null
   773→                    )
   774→                } catch (e: Exception) {
   775→                    log.warn("Failed to map vulnerability: {}", e.message, e)
   776→                    null
   777→                }
   778→            }
   779→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   780→            log.error("Failed to map CrowdStrike response to DTOs", e)
   781→            throw CrowdStrikeError.ServerError(message = "Failed to parse API response: ${e.message}", cause = e)
   782→        }
   783→    }

   785→    /**
   786→     * Check vulnerabilities against active exceptions
   787→     *
   788→     * Task: T025 [US1-Impl]
   789→     *
   790→     * @param vulnerabilities List of vulnerabilities
   791→     * @return Updated list with exception status
   792→     */
   793→    private fun checkExceptions(vulnerabilities: List<CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityDto>): List<CrowdStrikeVulnerabilityDto> {
   794→        log.debug("Checking {} vulnerabilities against active exceptions", vulnerabilities.size)
   795→
   796→        // Load all active exceptions
   797→        val activeExceptions = vulnerabilityExceptionRepository.findAll()
   798→            .filter { it.isActive() }
   799→
   800→        if (activeExceptions.isEmpty()) {
   801→            log.debug("No active exceptions found")
   802→            return vulnerabilities
   803→        }
   804→
   805→        // Check each vulnerability against exceptions
   806→        return vulnerabilities.map { vuln ->
   807→            val matchingException = activeExceptions.firstOrNull { exception ->
   808→                // IP-based exception
   809→                if (exception.exceptionType == com.secman.domain.VulnerabilityException.ExceptionType.IP) {
   810→                    vuln.ip == exception.targetValue
   811→                }
   812→                // Product-based exception
   813→                else if (exception.exceptionType == com.secman.domain.VulnerabilityException.ExceptionType.PRODUCT) {
   814→                    vuln.affectedProduct?.contains(exception.targetValue, ignoreCase = true) == true
   815→                } else {
   816→                    false
   817→                }
   818→            }
   819→
   820→            if (matchingException != null) {
   821→                log.debug("Vulnerability {} matches exception: {}", vuln.id, matchingException.reason)
   822→                vuln.copy(
   823→                    hasException = true,
   824→                    exceptionReason = matchingException.reason
   825→                )
   826→            } else {
   827→                vuln
   828→            }
   829→        }
   830→    }
